Title: flashback
Chapter_Text:
"Does it hurt a lot?" Shubman asked, his eyebrows knitted in concern.

"N-not unbearable," Ishan lied, swallowing a groan as a jolt of pain shot up his spine.

"God, Ish, why did you have to do a drill you never do before a matchday?" Shubman huffed, attempting to massage Ishan's back. He pulled his hands away as Ishan inhaled sharply, clearly in pain. “Seriously, how are you going to play like this?”

Ishan shook his head rapidly, staring hard at the ceiling. “I can, Iwill.”

“Ish, you can rest for one game, you know?” Shubman could see right through Ishan’s act.

“Not if that one game is the fucking final, Shubman,” Ishan snapped. “The scouts are going to be there. My fifties, my hundreds, they won't matter if I don't perform tomorrow.”

Shubman gritted his teeth in agitation. “Okay, you push yourself tomorrow. Then what? You risk being out of the game for months. Your body can't handle it, Ish.”

“You don't get to decide what my body can handle,” Ishan retorted, his voice edged with defiance as he glared back.

Shubman wanted to argue but seeing his friend in pain, he simply sighed. “Just get some rest for now, alright?”

Ishan continued glaring at the wall, unable to face away from Shubman even if he wished to, due to the persistent backache. The latter carefully settled beside him, slowly draping his arms around Ishan’s torso.

“I just want you to take care of yourself, Ish,” he said softly.

“Not at the price of leaving my team,” Ishan grumbled.

Shubman smiled, reaching to caress Ishan’s cheek gently. “You think it wouldn't cost us if you were to hurt yourself? Idiot.”

Ishan couldn't come up with a response, so he closed his eyes, pretending to sleep. He just hoped he'd be in form the next day.

He wasn't.

Each time he had to twist his waist to hit a boundary in practice, a sharp jolt of pain surged through him, and he’d let out a small groan.

"Coach, he can't possibly play like this," Shubman insisted. “Why don't you stop him?”

Coach sighed. “You think I haven't tried? He is the most stubborn boy I’ve ever come across.”

A sense of unease settled in his chest, a nagging feeling that something was about to go wrong. It wasn't just about the match. He was worried about Ishan, who had been pushing himself to the limit for months, ignoring Shubman’s warnings. The boy was clearly on the brink of a breakdown, and Shubman had no idea how to help him.

“Gill,” Coach turned to him with serious eyes. “Ichoseyou to be the Vice Captain because I trusted you with this team, okay?”

Shubman wasn't sure why Coach thought it was a good time to bring that up but he nodded anyway, his eyes still fixed on a struggling Ishan in the distance.

“Some decisions are hard but you have to make them for the team,” was all Coach said before leaving.

Shubman stood there, feeling his heart weighing down on him. He winced as Ishan dropped his bat and instinctively grabbed his back, as if itburnt. Shubman's immediate impulse was to rush to his friend's side, but he stopped in his tracks when he saw Ishan fight back angry tears.

He’s in pain. He’s in pain. He’s in pain.

That was all Shubman’s mind could repeat as dashed out of there. It was going to be the hardest decision of his life.

Ishan gritted his teeth, stifling a scream as he wrapped the compression bandage tightly around his waist. The pressure provided a sense of security, but the pain still throbbed relentlessly.

“God, please don't do this to me,” he mumbled, feeling a feverish heat coursing through his body.

It was almost time for the toss. He hastily fixed his collar, taking a deep breath before stepping out of the dressing room.

“Ishan!”

He turned at the sound of his name, frowning at the sight of a panicked Rishabh, his eyes wide with alarm.

“What, did you lose your bat again?”

Rishabh didn't even have the heart to shake his head. “Ish, your- your name’s n-not on the playing eleven.”

Ishan blinked. “What the hell are you on about?”

Rishabh walked in hesitantly. “They were announcing the players for both the teams and yours wasn’t among them.”

“They might have missed it,” Ishan said, though a cold feeling of dread formed a knot in his stomach.

“Who misses the Captain’s name, Ishan?” Rishabh looked angry. He should be too. Right. Why wasn't he angry?

“The toss,” he shakily muttered, turning his gaze to the clock. “I-I should go.”

“Shubman went.”

Ishan's eyes widened. “What?”

Rishabh swallowed hard, averting his gaze. “Shubman... he went for the toss.”

Ishan didn't know how to react to that. He remained standing, frozen, until his mind spoke:

Traitor.

Ishan had always had a complicated relationship with himself. Maybe he lived too much in his head. He knew that if he let his thoughts control him, he would end up hurting both himself and those around him. “Hurt people hurt people,” Shubman had once told him. Ishan had laughed it off, asking where he'd picked up all those edgy lines.

But at times like this, when Ishan felt like he was stripped of his defenses, he did not see his best friend. He did not see the boy who meant to him more than his own insecurities. He did not see hisShubi.

He saw Shubman Gill. The Vice Captain of the team. The Prince. The better choice.

Ishan hadn't realized he was already standing face-to-face with him.

“Why?”

Shubman almost flinched at the harsh tone before clenching his fists. "Let's talk it out later," he replied slowly, his voice strained.

“Why am I benched, Shubman?” he demanded.

“I said let’s talk later-”

Ishan wouldn't have any of it. He closed the gap between them, poking Shubman's chest as he spoke, his frustration evident in every word. “Who gave you the right to decide this?”

“You’re injured. I’m allowed to make the call, you know it.”

“I’m not fucking injured,” Ishan snapped. “I can play just fine. Why do you always act like you know everything about me?”

Shubman scowled. “I’m doing what's required for the team, Ishan. Don't be so fucking selfish.”

Ishan felt a dry laugh bubble in his chest. What had he not given for this team? And for what? To be calledselfishby his own best friend?

“There’s a limit to being petty, Shubman. Can you not see me winning for once?”

“What are you talking about?” Shubman shot him an incredulous look.

Ishan’s gaze sharpened. “You know what my friends used to say? That I shouldn't trust you too much, that you always had your eyes on the captaincy, that you’d snatch it away from me someday. But to think you would wait for the perfect day–”

“Have you fucking lost it?” Shubman yelled as he grabbed Ishan by his collar. “Do you even hear yourself, Ishan?”

“What, am I wrong?” Ishan snarled back, his anger fueling his words. “Youknewthe scouts would be here today. Who else but you knows how long I’ve waited for this day? How could you do this to me, you–”

“Is it my fault that you injured yourself?”

“I’m not–”

“Don’t lie!” Shubman snapped, cutting Ishan off. “You can't even run properly. How do you plan to hit boundaries, or dive for a run? You think you're that good, huh?”

Ishan shot him a glare, pushing Shubman’s hands off him. “As if people haven't played with an injury before. Dhoni played the World Cup–”

“Well, you’re not Dhoni! You’ll never be,” Shubman spat back. “So, just give up, alright? Playing today isn't going to change anything. In this state, you’re going to get out on your first ball and ruin it for all of us.”

Ishan's breath caught in his throat, his anger boiling over. "Fuck you, Shubman. This isn't how you’re supposed to act.”

“How should I act, pray tell?" he huffed. "How long do you expect me to indulge your immature decisions? If you can't take care of yourself, I couldn't care less about you either. You're acting like a fucking brat, Ishan. Not everything goes your way, why can't you accept that?”

Ishan felt a tremble in his hands as anger and hurt surged through him. He clenched his fists to stop it. “I’m immature, fine. What about you, Shubman? What have you done for the team except for sucking up to Coach? When have you declined every time you got favored over someone else?” He barked out a humorless laugh, adding, “You’re a narcissistic piece of shit. Everyone knows that, it’s me who always believed otherwise. Becausebest friends, right?”

This couldn't be Ishan. No matter how angry he got, he had never lashed out at Shubman like this before. Neverhurthim like this before.

Shubman's expression hardened, his jaw tightening as Ishan's words hit home. “If you’re going to be so insecure because of me, then so be it. I can't talk sense into someone who’s blinded by jealousy. I hope you know that the only reason they chose you as the Captain was because I stepped down myself.”

Ishan's fist shot forward instinctively before he could realize, but Abhishek held him back. “What the fuck are you guys doing?” He exclaimed, breaking them apart.

Ishan stumbled back, his eyes fleeting around nervously as he watched Abhishek check for any wounds on Shubman's face.

“Y-You knew it too?” Ishan muttered.

"Ish, it's not what you're thinking. We care about you," Abhishek said, his eyes softening.

But Ishan shook his head in disbelief. "No, no. Y-You're lying. You're all liars. Friends don’t hurt friends," he muttered, his voice cracking a little.

Shubman’s heart lurched, but he forced himself to stay composed. He wasn't sure if he could talk to Ishan without them screaming their heads off, so he let Abhishek steer the situation. He was better with words anyway.

Abhishek walked closer to the smaller boy, trying to make him understand. “Ish, listen to me please.”

Ishan stepped back. “Are you on his side?”

Shubman scoffed. “Sides? A fucking child, I told you.”

Ishan's eyes narrowed, the hurt evident in his gaze as he looked at Abhishek, silently pleading. “Abhi.”

“We don't have to do this, Ish. Let's get you some rest–”

"Traitor," Ishan snarled. "You're a traitor too. I can't trust anyone, no one," he mumbled to himself before turning on his heel and fleeing from there.

“Why did you have to trigger him?” Abhishek asked Shubman with a sigh.

“You don't know how much his words stabbed,” Shubman said with a snort. “There's something off about him. He just loses it sometimes.”

“Don’t make me hit you,” Abhishek warned.

Shubman rubbed his temples, feeling the strain from all the yelling. “Let’s just go and get this shit over with. I’ll talk to Ishan after we win.”

Ishan did not run down to the ground with the others when Shubman hit the winning six. He wasn't there with them when they lifted the trophy. Instead, he watched them from the dressing room, forcing himself to be happy for his team. He watched Coach praise Shubman proudly. He watched Shubman's triumphant smile as he hugged everyone, acting like the Captain they probably deserved.

Pathetic. Loser. Unworthy.

Ishan wasn't sure if he could fight his thoughts anymore. Where did it all go wrong? How far had he fallen that he now couldn't bear the sight of his own best friend? Maybe Shubman was right. Hewasselfish.

“Feel like shit yet?”

Ishan jerked at the voice, turning to see the man he had never spoken to. He knew him though. Everyone knew Shastri, as some sort of a cryptic genius at least.

Shastri spoke again when Ishan remained silent. “You might be the first Captain in years to have been benched in the final.”

Ishan snorted. “Oh, yeah. I definitely feel like shitnow.”

Shastri shot him an amused smirk. “Also the first Captain in a decade to win your school the U-17 league.”

Ishan roughed his hair in agitation. “Do I look like a fucking Captain to you right now? It doesn't matter anymore. People were right, my pride is worthless.”

“Then prove them wrong.”

Ishan looked up. “Huh?”

"Kids these days act like life ends at eighteen," Shastri said with a scoff. “Is there anything like worthless pride? Maybe.”

“Is there a ‘but’?” Ishan asked in a small voice.

Shastri smiled. “Butdon't you ever let them forget your worthless pride.”

Ishan studied the man’s expression, looking for any sign of pity, but there wasn't any. “Why are you here? You’re Shubman’s personal coach, aren't you?”

Shastri took a swig of his diet coke that happened to have magically appeared in his hand. “I’m also one of the scouts today.”

Ishan’s eyes widened in surprise as he stood up abruptly. “R-Really? Is Shubman getting selected?”

Ishan couldn't figure out if it was hope or resentment that bubbled in the pit of his stomach. Shastri waved dismissively, “It’s not much of a competition when there’s Virat and Rohit. We pick our players halfway through the season, boy. Does it make you feel any better now?”

“Hell no. It makes me mad,” Ishan grunted.

“That’s a good sign,” Shastri laughed as he gazed out at the ground. “Gill, he’s going toMeridian.”

Ishan snapped his head up, his heart racing with a mix of surprise and disappointment. Shubman hadn't mentioned anything about it. Sure, both of them had applied for one of the most prestigious sports institutes around but Shubman never told him he got in. Probably because Ishan hadn't received his acceptance letter yet.

“Oh,” was all Ishan could say.

Shastri looked back at the boy. “Come toSummit, Kishan.”

“Are you offering to train me?” Ishan asked with a frown. He knew Shastri wasn't one to go around advertising, so there must be a catch.

"I couldn't care less about all that anymore," Shastri snorted. "I'm just waiting for my retirement, then I'm gonna wander the mountains, smoke weed with the ascetics or something."

If Ishan wasn't so caught up in his emotions, he would have been impressed by the man's sheer ability to not give any fucks.

“Why do you want me to come then?” He asked.

Shastri sounded genuine for once when he said, “Summithas never won a final.”

Ishan felt a small ignition behind his eyes. “You think I can make them win?”

Shastri paused as he stared at the boy before smiling, as if he saw something beyond Ishan’s tear-streaked cheeks that he had rubbed harshly enough to make sure no one noticed.

“You think you can't?”

Ishan knew what he had to do. He was going to let his thoughts take over.

So he stood before his two best friends, a steely stoic glint in his eyes. “Congrats.”

Abhishek tried to stop Shubman from sprouting anything stupid, but the tension in the air was palpable.

“That's all?” Shubman sneered. “You didn't show uponce. The boys, Coach, your parents, everyone was waiting for you. That’s messed up, Ishan.”

“I know you're upset,” Abhishek spoke softly. “But things happen in sports, you know.”

“Well, I’m not going to accept as things are. I’m fuckingstubbornand I’ll hold onto that,” he said with a pointed look at Shubman.

“Fine. What are you going to do then? Whatcanyou do, Ishan?” Shubman retorted, growing frustrated by the second.

“I’ll win,” Ishan replied calmly. “I’ll win the college league. I’ll defeat you if that's the last thing I do.”

Shubman wanted to laugh but he felt like he would end up crying. He was tired, so tired of everything.Of Ishan. His love was worn out. His love wasn't enough to save his best friend. So, he didn't shout. He didn't ask what had happened to their promise of playing together. To their dreams of joining the national team someday. Tothem.

Abhishek's grip tightened on Ishan's shoulder as he yelled, “What aboutme?Why do you never think about me? Do you really think we would betray you?Iwould betray you? What have I done to not earn your trust even after so many years, Ishan? You’re fucking hurting me, both of you!”

Shubman felt a lump form in the back of his throat, unshed tears threatening to spill. "Fuck this. Fuck you both," he muttered. "I’m not going to run myself over for your charity friendship, Ishan.”

Ishan’s stomach churned with guilt, but he knew he had to do this. If he didn't, his thoughts would consume him. He couldn't always hide away in Shubman’s hugs. He had to do something for himself. One day, Shubman was going to see howweakhe was, and leave him for good. Ishan couldn't let that happen. So, he was going to leave first.

He turned to Abhishek, who looked shaken up from all that was happening. “Whose side are you on?”

“Will you just fucking stop?!” Shubman yelled.

But Ishan kept his gaze fixed on Abhishek, who looked utterly heartbroken. “Shubman,” he mumbled.

Ishan simply nodded while Shubman widened his eyes nervously. “Abhi, you don't have to do this. L-Let's just slap some sense into him and it’ll be okay, alright?”

Abhishek released his grip from Ishan slowly, turning to Shubman. “Don't you see it? He’s not our Ish anymore.”

Shubman watched as Ishan turned away, walking out of the room. “Stop him, Abhi. He’ll listen to you, fucking stop him-”

“Shubman,” Abhishek said sharply. “Let him go.”

“No, he’s overthinking again. His mind is scary, it got to him. Please, we need to stop him.” The tears finally rolled down his face as he sobbed.

“We can't protect him forever, Shub. I think he knows what he’s doing,” Abhishek said, trying to sound calmer than he felt.

“What the fuck is he doing?” Shubman mumbled into Abhishek's shoulder as he fell into a hug.

Abhishek smiled through his own tears as he rubbed circles on Shubman’s back. “Hedoingthis to put a stop to his thoughts,” he murmured softly. “He will come back, Shub. He’ll come back stronger than ever.”

It's not that they stopped trying to contact Ishan. It was just that their hearts broke every time the hopeful ring turned into “The number you have dialed is no longer in service.”

Title: CH: 13
Chapter_Text:
a/n:jeetne ki khushi mein update. today MI was MI-ing. no downfall will stop me from supporting them. and ishan meri jaan, you were a beauty today <3

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Ishan woke up in a cold sweat, his heart pounding in his chest. He rubbed his temples with a groan, the harsh lights stinging his eyes. “Fuck, am I dead,” he muttered.

“Not yet.”

Ishan blinked at the sight of Aditi beside him. “Am I dreaming?”

“No,” she chuckled softly, handing him a glass of water.

Ishan mentally cussed, realizing she had probably seen him sprawled on the bed like a dying seal for god knows how long. Clearing his throat awkwardly, he sat up. “What’re you doing here?”

“Would you rather wake up to someone else?” she teased.

Ishan flushed a little. “Um, that’s not what I– How long have I been out?”

She didn't answer. Instead she crossed her arms, a concerned furrow appearing on her brow. “You know, Ishan, you really had us worried.”

“Sorry, I just…” he trailed off, rubbing his neck.

“What are you sorry for, silly? For being selfless?”

Ishan winced, feeling a pang of unease. “I’m not sure if that fits me,” he mumbled.

“Hm?”

“Nothing. H-How’s the match going?”

Ishan’s voice wavered with jitters. What if they were losing? Or worse, what if they were losing because ofhim?Besides, he had a promise to fulfill– the one he made to Shubman three years ago.

Oh, right. He remembered Shubman shouting his name before he blacked out. “Who brought me here?” he asked, finally glancing around the medical room.

"Tilak," Aditi replied. "The timeout ended, so the others couldn't leave the ground.”

"I see," Ishan muttered. “Poor Tillu, the boy’s barely got any flesh on him.”

Aditi chuckled softly as she looked for the cold packs. Ishan watched her quietly, his mind still reeling from what happened. What were the odds of history repeating itself on a day he'd desperately wished it wouldn't? The weight of knowing he was responsible for his own injury both times sat on his shoulder like the eagle pecking out Prometheus’s liver.

“Earth to, Ishan?” Aditi's voice broke through his thoughts as she waved her hands in front of his face. “You've been a little lost lately. What's the matter, Ish?”

Ishan’s breath came out labored, his body hunched over as his hands clenched tightly around his head, fingers digging into his scalp. “I feel pathetic, Adi.”

Aditi softened at how vulnerable he looked. She moved closer to him, gently patting his back. “I’m listening, tell me what's been bothering you.”

He peeked at her from behind his hands, half expecting to see a look of pity or charitable sympathy. But there was onlyaffection. Ishan had never spoken out loud about what happened, not even to Mayank. Everyone had a vague idea about how him and Shubman fell out, but no one knew the hurt that came along with it. He fleeted his gaze around the walls, fearing that Aditi too would see the weakness he had so long tried to hide from her. “Do you know that I never played the U-17 final?” he asked, swallowing a lump in his throat.

“Yes, I heard,” she replied, nodding slowly. “Have you been thinking about the past?”

Ishan shifted on the bed uneasily, finding the right words. “I injured myself the day before the final… just like now. I don't know why it happened again.”

Aditi smiled softly, nodding along as he continued. “I think I was so scared of it happening again– and I was trying so hard to prevent it–thatit inadvertently ended up happening again. Sorry, am I making sense?”

“You are. It happens sometimes; some kind of fear-desire paradox, I guess,” she replies with a weak laugh. “Ish, I want you to know that making mistakes is okay.”

“But-!” Ishan almost exclaimed, but caught himself before he let out a frustrated sigh. “Two times, Adi. How can I fuck up the same twice? And it cost me more than just the match. It cost me… everything.”

He blinked back the tears threatening to spill. Fuck, he didn't want to break down in front of her. “I’m sick of myself, Adi. Ireallytried this time, I did. No one has hurt me like I have,” he whispered shakily. He glanced at his hands that have gotten rougher in the past few weeks with all the relentless practice.

Aditi couldn't bear the sight of theirbrightestboy hurting. He didn't deserve to feel this way, not when all he had ever done was make others feelworthy. “Ishan,” she smiled, prompting him to look up.

“Let it go,” she said.

Ishan stared at her through his blurry vision. He waited for her to continue but that was all she said.What? What do I let go? What if it leaves claw marks?It was the worst time to remember something his high school English teacher, in a class that Ishan found particularly uninteresting even by his standards, had said.“When you’re born in a burning house, you think the whole world is on fire. But it’s not.”Maybe that’s what it was. Ishan hadn’t known anything other than his regrets for a long time. He had built a home in them.

“Ish,” Aditi finally spoke again. “Forgive yourself.”

Ishan was crying before he realized. His hands quivered like a prayer, spread over his body like mercy. Then it justclicked. The answer was right before his eyes all along. He was just not brave enough to ask the question.

What do you do when you fail, again, and again, and again?

You forgive yourself, again, and again, and again.

He looked up at Aditi, who hadn’t stopped smiling. “What if I don’t?” he asked.

Her smile only widened. “Time will pass anyways.”

Ishan's laughter bubbled forth, genuine and unbridled. "You're right," he conceded with a chuckle, wiping away his tears. "So I might as well."  It was as odd as it was not, how simple life was. Just like cricket. What choice was there but to keep going,despite?Win or lose, what mattered was to never give up.

“What do you regret the most, Ish?” Aditi asked, breaking the comfortable silence that had stretched on.

What did he regret the most? Was it injuring himself? Or was it not being good enough? Or was it blaming Shubman? Or was it abandoning Abhishek? Or was it just being a seventeen year old boy with dreams?

"I….regret,” Ishan felt his mouth go dry. “I regret not being there with my team that day, when they won– whenwewon. I regret not running down to the ground, even with my injury, and crying with them instead of crying alone in the dressing room. It wouldn't have mattered that we were crying for different reasons if we weretogether.”

Aditi smiled. It didn't look sad. It was one of her beautiful smiles that Ishan would often remember on random afternoons, gazing at the sunset. “Then do it now,” she said.

“H-Huh?”

“You asked me how long you were out, right?”

Ishan raised an eyebrow, his head tilted in confusion. Aditi added, “Long enough for us to win.”

Ishan's eyes widened, a small gasp escaping his lips. “Please tell me you’re joking–”

“I’m not!” Aditi giggled happily. “We did it, Ish!”

All Ishan could do was cry again. This time though, happy tears. He quickly rose to his feet, his eyes gleaming with mirth. Suddenly, it didn’t matter who won them the match. It didn’t matter if he couldn’t play. It didn’t matter if he couldn’t see Shubman’s face asMeridianlost. All that mattered was that theywon.

“God, I think I’m gonna pass out again,” he exclaimed.

“Not before you kiss the trophy!”

Ishan spun around to see his teammates, immediately jumping into their midst. He reached out to ruffle Rishabh's hair proudly, who stood tall holding the trophy. Ishan's heart swelled at the sight of it. “She’s gorgeous,” he whispered as the others chuckled.

“It’s finally yours.”

Ishan shook his head before pecking the cool surface of the trophy. “Ours.”

“O Captain! My Captain!” Surya sang, as everyone cheered.

Rohit swatted Ishan’s head gently when the boy looked like he was about to tear up. “Tilak and Siraj cried enough on everyone's behalf,” he said, pulling the boy in a tight hug. Everyone stared fondly as Ishan’s breathy laughs filled the room. Tilak and Siraj latched onto their Captain like leeches and bawled out their eyes yet again.

"Guess who took Gill’s catch," Mayank said with a smirk. Then, before Ishan could respond, he blurted out, "Abhishek!"

Ishan's smile wavered slightly, but he quickly covered it up with a chuckle. His eyes searched for the boy in the huddle, but couldn't find him. Long after their celebratory dances and group selfies, he slipped away to look for him. Sure enough, Ishan spotted him, quietly watching Shubman's post-match interview from a distance.

Ishan hesitated as he inched closer towards the boy. Abhishek’s shoulders looked slumped from behind, like he was trying to make himself smaller. Ishan clenched his fists, breathing deeply, as he whispered to himself, “Let it go.”

Abhishek yelped in surprise as someone suddenly leaped onto his back, nearly knocking the wind out of him. The only reason why he didn't drop the person was because he knew who it was. He had done this more times than he could count. His hands shot out and grasped the other’s calves as easily as breathing. He knew that giggle tickling his ears like the back of his hand.

"Ish," he spoke in his softest voice.

"Don't you think you should check on someone who passed out?" Ishan pouted.

"I thought you wouldn't want to see me," Abhishek mumbled.

Ishan's heart broke a little at that. He raised his hand and smacked Abhishek, prompting a whine from him. “Yeah, I would never want to see you if you say something stupid like that again,” he huffed.

Abhishek chuckled weakly, rocking lightly on his feet. “Okay, I’m sorry. How are you feeling now?”

“Hm, very proud.” Ishan rubbed his cheek against Abhishek’s affectionately, causing the other to giggle as well. “I’m so proud of you boys.”

“Especially me, right?” Abhishek grinned.

“Especiallyfor taking Shubi’s catch.”

“Oh, you should have seen his face! So satisfying.”

Ishan guffawed, throwing his head back. “Man, I missed it. I can finally gloat to him for the rest of his life.”

Abhishek hummed softly as they gazed at the boy in the distance. While his smile seemed strained, he didn't appear as distraught as Ishan would have been if they had lost.

Later, when Ishan was planting a kiss on Abhishek’s cheek as he got piggybacked around, Shubman rushed to them, letting out a dramatic gasp. “How many betrayals tonight, Abhi?!”

Ishan rolled his eyes playfully, then leaned in to deliberately kiss the boy's other cheek. Shubman let out a small squeal, reaching over to rub Abhishek's face with the hem of his sleeve. “No PDA if I can't join!”

Abhishek chuckled, pushing away Shubman. “Shoo, I’m having some quality time with my best friend,” he said, sticking out his tongue.

Shubman pouted, puffing out his cheeks. Then, with a grin, he jumped onto them, causing them to tumble down together. Hearty laughs filled the air around them as they lay on the ground, gazing up at the night sky.

“Do you remember when I had my leg fractured in middle school?” Ishan asked.

“I definitely remember Shub crying like you were about to die,” Abhishek said with a snicker.

Shubman swatted his arm, huffing. “I was worried, okay? He was so small, I thought his body was more fragile than others!”

Ishan shot him a glare before continuing, “I couldn't play for so long, and I used to sulk around all day. I couldn't even watch Dhoni play because my hands would itch to get on my gloves, and then mom would scold me.”

Shubman scrunched his nose, remembering howemptyit felt to not have Ishan around in practice. “Then we lied to you that we weren't playing either, so that you would feel better.”

Ishan sighed. “Yeah, but I could never let that happen so I told you guys to- Wait, you lied?!”

Shubman erupted into fits of giggles as Ishan playfully tickled him, and Abhishek joined in. "Would the loser like to say anything?" Ishan asked with a smirk, his fingers still dancing along Shubman's sides.

Shubman huffed, attempting to swat Ishan's hand away amidst his laughter. "But you didn't even play," he replied before he could fully catch his breath. The regret followed instantly when Ishan stilled, his hands falling by his sides.

Shubman wished Ishan would slap him, punch him, anything to alleviate the guilt gnawing at him. His heart clenched as he shut his eyes, bracing himself for Ishan's reaction.Why am I so stupid?He did not have the heart to look at those hurt eyes, not when he’d spent the past few weeks trying to win Ishan’s trust again.

“Low blow, Shub,”Abhishek thought as he nervously glanced at Ishan, ready to hold him down if the boy tried to bolt.

Shubman was forced to open his eyes when Ishan tightened his grip on his collar. “Ish, I’m–”

“I guess I didn't even need to play to beat you. That's how good my team is,” Ishan said with a grin.

Shubman gaped, not sure how to respond. Abhishek finally chuckled, slinging an arm around Ishan. “Damn right! Sucks to lose, eh?”

A short laugh escaped Shubman’s lips, turning into a relieved one. “Whatever, I won the POTT this time,” he beamed.

“I took pity on you,” Ishan said, ruffling his hair.

Shubman could die happily tonight if you asked. He wasn't sure what changed and he didn't dare ask. Whatever it was, he was thankful to have his Ishan back. He pulled the smaller boy in a desperate hug, holding him tightly. “I missed you,” he whispered.

Ishan’s expression softened as he rubbed the other’s back affectionately. This was it.Home. “I missed you too,” Ishan whispered back before pulling Abhishek into the hug as well.

If life ever gives you a second chance, grab it like the hand of an old friend.

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Title: CH: 14
Chapter_Text:
Shubman was annoyed at the very least, if his puffed cheeks and scowl were anything to go by.

"What's got your panties in a twist?" Yashasvi quipped, sparing him a sideways glance.

"Nothing," Shubman replied calmly.

Yashasvi concluded that it was, in fact, something. But he couldn't be bothered to entertain one of Shubman's man-periods, especially when they had just lost the final. Nevertheless, he followed the boy's gaze to the skipper ofSummit, who was being carried around, princess-style, by someone for some reason.

Yashasvi rested a comforting hand on Shubman's shoulder. "I will avenge you next year."

"Huh?" Shubman turned to him. "Oh. Yeah, of course."

Yashasvi continued to talk about the plans he already had for the next season, which Shubman would have liked to listen to, being the good senior he was, if his mind wasn't preoccupied by a certain giggly boy who seemed to be having way too much fun without him.

It was when their eyes met for the third time across the ground and Ishan swiftly averted his gaze that Shubman decided to stride over to him, leaving Yashasvi's mouth agape.

Yashasvi blinked at the retreating back. "The only person I can stand in this whole darn team is you," he grumbled to the person next to him.

"I know!" Dhruv beamed.

Yashasvi stared at his perpetually smiley friend. "Too bright," he mumbled, grabbing both of their kits before announcing a race to the dressing room.

Shubman stood unimpressed as he walked into Mayank demonstrating his bulking muscles by lifting Ishan for everyone to see.

"Ish, can we talk?" he called out rather loudly.

"Sure," Ishan replied, signaling for Mayank to put him down. Mayank brushed off the tension in the air and chased after Abhishek to lift him up too, much to the other's embarrassment.

"What's up?" Ishan asked once they were a few yards away from everyone.

"You tell me what's up," Shubman's pout returned. "You say you missed me, and then you go around hanging out with others. Shouldn't you give me all your attention after, oh I don't know, THREE YEARS?"

Ishan suppressed a smile, clearing his throat. "But we've been meeting for a while now, Shubman."

"Don't call me that!" Shubman exclaimed, smacking his ass.

"Oi," Ishan hissed. "You've grown so-!"

Shubman smirked, leaning in closer. "Big?" he whispered.

"Perverted!" Ishan huffed. Shubman giggled as he draped an arm around the boy, grinning from ear to ear when the other didn't push him away.

"Ishhh," he drawled.

"Hm?"

"Spend the night with me."

Ishan shot him an amused look. "To do what?"

"Anything," Shubman wiggled his eyebrows suggestively. Ishan stared at him until both of them bursted out laughing.

"I'm serious," Shubman whined. "We haven't cuddled in ages!"

"Grown ass man," Ishan muttered, rolling his eyes.

"What, am I too old for your affection now?" Shubman shot him those irresistible puppy-dog eyes.

Ishan sighed. "Shubi, I'm rooming with Mayank-"

Shubman scowled, crossing his arms. "Oh, you can't stay away from yournew best friendfor one night, is that it?"

"Stop acting like you hate him, Shubi. How much longer will you cling to that grudge over a jersey-"

"It was a limited edition, signed jersey! OfSachin!" Shubman argued. "And why didn't you deny it when I called him your best friend?" he added, gasping dramatically.

Ishan was so done with this boy. He simply walked away after shooting him an eye-roll. Shubman chuckled and followed him, nagging him to let him stay over.

"The puppy is back," Rishabh remarked as he stuffed his face with ice cream (courtesy of Shastri). Tilak nodded, though he couldn't see any puppy in sight.

Ishan narrowed his eyes as he watched Shubman lock the door. "No funny business, Gill," he warned.

Shubman shrugged innocently. "Just making sure monkey- oops. I mean, Mayank doesn't barge in."

Ishan sighed, lacking the energy to argue at midnight. Mayank could crash with someone else; he'd get the memo.

Ishan could feel eyes on him as he pulled his shirt off. He put on a good show of not noticing as Shubman drew closer. Only when Shubman's fingers reached for Ishan's hair did he meet the other's gaze.

"Will you come out of the shower smelling like vanilla again?" Shubman asked.

Ishan nodded slowly, puzzled. "W-Why?"

"It's suffocating," Shubman said, his eyes tracing the shape of Ishan's lips.

"Oh, sorry-"

"I like it."

When Ishan tilted his head in confusion, Shubman added, "I want to be suffocated by your scent. It calms my nerves."

After a pause, Ishan replied, "You know, sometimes you creep me out." He gently pushed Shubman away and made his way towards the bathroom.

Shubman chuckled and settled onto Ishan's bed, his gaze drifting out of the window. He wrapped his arms around one of the pillows, holding it close to his chest. After ten minutes of pretending it was Ishan, and showering it with peppery kisses, Shubman started counting stars in the sky.

Ishan ended up spending more time than usual in the shower. As he stood before the mirror, he stared at his own reflection, the fog slowly dissipating. He rubbed the wetness on his palms against the towel. He made sure to bring his change of clothes with him, not willing to risk any more nudity.

As Ishan stepped out of the bathroom, the room was dark, lit only by the soft moonlight filtering in through the window. "Shubi?" he called out, as he approached the bed.

Shubman hummed softly before pulling Ishan down, enveloping the other's back with his own chest. He nestled his nose into the crook of Ishan's neck, planting a feathery kiss on the boy's shoulder.

"Why are you sitting in the dark?" Ishan asked, rubbing circles on Shubman's knee.

"You smell nice," Shubman replied instead.

Ishan bit back a smile. "Stop sniffing me," he nudged.

The silence that followed was comforting. With Shubman, Ishan could simply be quiet. He could let go and justexist. You can find a lot of people to talk to, but not many to be silent with.

It took Ishan some time to notice the wetness on his shoulder. "Why are you crying, idiot?" he murmured softly.

"I'm not an idiot," Shubman sniffled in response. He wrapped his hands around Ishan's ribs, pulling him closer than he already was. "I'm scared that this is all a dream. What if I wake up tomorrow and you go back to hating me?"

"Should I pinch you real hard?" Ishan teased, eliciting a small whine from Shubman.

"Okay, geez. You're such a sap, Shubi. You know I'd never truly hate you. I was just... upset," Ishan reassured him.

"For three years?" Shubman huffed.

Ishan turned around slightly, the tip of his nose grazing against Shubman's cheek. "Sometimes you have to give up things you love, that is the price ofgrowing."

Shubman remained quiet for a moment before finally mumbling, "Was it that easy to give me up?"

Ishan bit the inside of his cheek, turning away as Shubman spoke with a hint of remorse, "I'm sorry. I haven't forgiven myself for not trying to stop you that day."

"It's not entirely your fault. I can't always depend on others," Ishan sighed. "It was only a matter of time before it happened again. I needed to stop running from myself at some point."

He gently wiped away Shubman's tears before pressing a light kiss on his cheek. He spoke before the other went on blubbering, "Do you really want to spend the night crying?"

Shubman let on a small smile. "Okay, then. Distract me."

"How so?" Ishan let out an amused huff, playing with Shubman's fingers idly.

Shubman gently grasped Ishan's jaw, tilting his head towards himself. "Can I?"

Ishan blinked. "What?"

Shubman's cheeks flushed (he mentally thanked the darkness).Did Ishan forget?

"Can I kiss you?"

"No!" Ishan yelped almost immediately.

Shubman's expression fell as his dog-ears drooped. "No?"

"Um," Ishan squirmed uneasily. "W-We shouldn't do it without any reason, right?"

"Hm, what was your reason back then?"

Ishan felt his ears get warm as Shubman's warm breath brushed against his face. "I don't know," he mumbled. It was to rile the other up. Probably.

"I don't know either." Shubman smiled, inching closer. "So do you want to...."

Ishan's eyes darted around nervously, he was ready to jump up and make a run for another shower when Shubman chuckled.

"Watch Star Wars?" he completed.

Ishan released a breath he hadn't realized he was holding. Then, he playfully slapped Shubman's cheek, only for the other to catch his hand and press a kiss on his knuckles. It wasn't anything odd for them. Shubman had first done it when Ishan had almost burnt his hand while trying to bake a cake for Shubman's birthday.

"Yeah," Ishan replied softly.

Shubman couldn't concentrate, not even drooling at Anakin's entrance, which earned him a pointed look from Ishan.

He was caught up mentally kicking himself for his stupidity. Why did he have to go and askthat?It was different when they wereself-proclaimed enemies. Maybe Ishan was right. There was no reason to do it again.

Still, Shubman couldn't help but wish there was.

"He locked me out!"

"Calm down, Mayank," Abhishek interrupted, rolling his eyes at the boy pacing angrily around his room. "It was probably Shubman."

"Exactly," Mayank huffed. "Who's he to-"

"Ishan's best friend."

"Ouch."

"Get used to it," Abhishek said with indifferent shrug.

"Yo, I wasn't being serious," Mayank let out a weak laugh.

Abhishek rubbed his neck awkwardly before managing a grin. "Let the lovebirds be. C'mon, let's watch-"

"Please don't say Star Wars."

Abhishek scrunched his nose, shaking his head. "Do you take me for a nerd?"

"Thank God," Mayank sighed in relief, flopping down next to the boy. "You would look cute in glasses though," he muttered after a while.

"Shut up," Abhishek retorted, ignoring the warmth in his cheeks.

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Title: CH: 15
Chapter_Text:
Shubman woke up to find Ishan curled up against his chest. He cautiously shifted away a bit, worried that his racing heart might wake Ishan from his sleep.

Unfortunately, he also noticed Mayank looming over him, glaring intently. Shubman almost shrieked.

“How did you get in?!” he shouted in a hushed voice.

“You think it’s my first time getting locked out of my own room?” Mayank retorted, rolling his eyes.

“Then why didn't you come last night?” Shubman asked, slowly getting out of bed, causing Ishan to stir in his sleep a little. He mentally cooed at the sight of Ishan wrapping his hands around the pillow habitually.

Darned pillow, getting to cuddle his best friend for three years. Tch.

“Thought I should give yourotherbest friend some company,” Mayank snorted. Was it purely for self-interest reasons? Yes. Did anyone need to know about it? No.

"Crap," Shubman muttered, realizing he might have ditched the boy last night. His face lit up with a mischievous grin as he asked, “Is he still sleeping?”

“Yeah. Why?”

“Abhi, I’m coming, baby!” Shubman sang dramatically before dashing out of the room, armed with the perfect idea to wake him up.

Mayank shrugged it off, then turned to a sleeping Ishan. The boy jolted awake when Mayank almost kicked him out of the bed.

"WHAT HAPPENED?" Ishan demanded, his eyes darting around nervously, as if scouring for any signs of a potential raid.

Mayank only smirked. “Race you to the mess,” he declared before taking off.

“Wait, what?” It took Ishan a moment to reboot his brain for the day before he exclaimed, “You fucker–!”

After freshening up and throwing on a shirt (did he sleep shirtless?), Ishan stomped down the hallway with an annoyed glare before he bumped into Aditi, who could easily be mistaken for an angel this early in the morning.

"Aren't you supposed to have today off?" Ishan asked, a smile quickly spreading across his lips.

Aditi was about to mention that she had some work with Rohit, but then she paused, her dimples popping off cheekily. “That's exactly why I’m here. Let’s go.”

Ishan rubbed his neck sheepishly. “Um, where to?”

“On a date.”

Ishan blinked, feeling a blush slowly creeping into his cheeks. “N-now? Just like that?”

“You got any problem?” Aditi asked, crossing her arms.

Ishan glanced down at himself, mentally cringing at his outfit. He sighed and smiled up at her. “No, ma’am.”

“Where’s Ishu baby?” Hardik asked, munching on a carrot.

“Not a baby anymore, Harry,” Rishabh said with a snicker. “He’s on a date.”

Surya whistled. “Finally.”

Shubman frowned, looking up from his plate. “What do you mean finally?”

"You see, Ishan has had this not-so-subtle crush on Aditi for almost a year now," Rishabh explained. "But he is a total wuss, so I guess she stepped up the game herself.”

"Oh." Shubman wasn't sure how to feel about his best friend not sharing his love life with him. To be fair, they hadn't been on talking terms for three years, but Ishan could have mentioned it when they started talking again a few weeks back!

“Aw, a girl snatched away your best fwend. Is wittle Shubi going to cry?” Mayank drawled in a mocking voice.

Glaring, Shubman threw a grape at him, which Mayank deftly caught in his mouth. “Whoisthis Aditi anyway?” he asked.

“Angel.”

“Sunshine.”

“Flower.”

“Our team manager,” Surya supplied after thehelpfulreplies from the others.

Shubman scrunched his face. “And she is intoIshan? Talk about compromise.”

“Liar,” Mayank muttered under his breath.

“Should we spy on them?” Yuzi asked, a mischievous glint in his eyes.

“That's so wrong,” Rishabh gasped. “We should absolutely do that.”

Jaddu snickered as they high-fived each other, causing Abhishek to groan. “Really guys? What are we, middle schoolers?”

Mayank considered for a second before siding with Yuzi. “I’m in for some blackmail material.”

Abhishek shook his head, turning to Hardik. “Vice-Cap, knock some sense into them, please.”

Hardik looked into the distance dramatically, declaring, “I must go and protect Ishu baby.”

Abhishek face-palmed before sighing. “Shub-”

"Guys, hurry up!" Shubman exclaimed happily, causing Abhishek to gape. He hadn't realized Shubman was already on his feet, ready to go.

“You’re getting nothing out of being mature here, hero.” Surya smiled, offering Abhishek a hand.

Abhishek couldn't help but laugh at the absurdity of the situation. “Yeah, I guess I could live a little.”

“No way,hehas a girlfriend too?” Rishabh gasped as they strolled around the campus park, judging couples.

They couldn't spot Ishan and Aditi, but they did manage to gather the latest gossip about who was dating whom and who was cheating on whom.

Shubman grew impatient, starting to wonder if they were at the wrong place. But where would they go so early anyway? He wanted to see Ishan’s reactions– how he blushed, smiled, and acted shy around someone he liked. Ishan never dated anyone in school, dedicating his soul and body to cricket. So what faces would he make while being kissed?

Oh, wait.

Shubman already knew that.

God, Shubman had kissed his best friend. His lips had touched Ishan's, and had trailed down the boy’s neck. It felt like a fever dream now.

He was busy blushing all by himself like an idiot when he heard Rishabh gasp. “Isn't that Tillu?” he spoke, pointing over to the boy who was few yards away.

“He hasotherfriends?” Mayank gaped, referring to a boy sitting next to Tilak. He had never seen Tilak talk so animatedly, causing his friend to listen to him fondly.

“Are they- Wait, are they holdinghands?” Surya pointed out.

Tilak waved them over as soon as he noticed them. “What’s up, guys?”

“Tillu, you made a friend!” Hardik slapped his back proudly.

Tilak chuckled, nodding brightly. “Isn’t he a total cutie?”

The boy blushed visibly. Cons of being white, probably.

“Aren’t you the exchange student who joined last year?” Surya asked, to which he nodded. “Man, you were the talk of the campus last year. New basketball prodigy ofSummit, I heard.”

“Great! Rohan can eat shit,” Hardik snorted. “What’s your name, White Boy?”

“This is DB,” Tilak beamed. “My boyfriend.”

Everyone stilled in stunned silence. For some reason, Shubman felt like he had to say something as a sense of unease settled on his chest. It was absurd because it's not like the situation was about him. His secret was still his, and Ishan’s. No one could know he was gay. Not if they were going to react like this. Shubman really wanted to say something before this Tilak guy, who wasn't evenhisteammate, watched his friends stare at him like strangers.

“Damn, Tillu got game!”

Rishabh was the first one to break the tension, followed by others with hoots and whistles. Hardik once again proudly slapped Tilak on the back. “You better treat him right, White Boy!”

DB chuckled, slinging an arm around Tilak. “Of course, mate.”

Only then did Shubman realize that Tilak hadn't stopped smiling, not even when everyone else paled like ghosts. How much trust did he have in his team? The knot of unease did not leave Shubman. How could Tilak be so open about it? How was he so brave? How had the world not broken this boy’s heart yet?

“Isn't it funny, though?” Abhishek spoke. “To think that Tilak found someone in the basketball team. I thought those guys were assholes.”

“Theyare. Just not him,” Tilak replied, looking at his boyfriend fondly. “Well, why else do you think they hated me?” he said with a scoff.

“You didn't try to keep it a secret, huh?”

“He’s way too beautiful to be a secret. Aren't you, DB?” he nudged his boyfriend, who grinned adorably.

Shubman felt a little sick. God, what kind of terrible human was he to not be able to see two guys happy together? Heenviedthem. He walked away from the group quietly to get some air, leaving them to tease the hell out of the two lovers.

"Since we won, now what?" Aditi asked, slightly trailing her fingers along the flower bed.

“I don’t know,” Ishan hummed. “We graduate in three months. What's really left to do now?”

Aditi turned to face him. “Aren’t you trying out for domestic?”

Ishan shot her a weak smile, rubbing his neck. “I couldn't play the final, Adi. I don't think the scouts would consider me.”

“One match doesn't define you,” Aditi protested. “You’ve performed incredibly all season. Believe in yourself, Ish.”

“I find that a little hard to do,” Ishan said with a wry chuckle.

“Then let me.”

“What?”

“Let me believe in you, Ishan. You won't let me down, right?”

Ishan stared at her for a second, wondering what he had done to deserve having her by his side. “Never,” he said with a genuine smile.

Aditi’s dimples peeked out again, prompting Ishan to reach out with his hand before he realized, stopping midway. He decided instead to tuck away a few strands of her hair, causing her to blush.

“Kindness looks beautiful on you,” he whispered.

“You make me kind, Ish,” she whispered back.

Ishan waited for his heart to thump loudly against his ribs as he dropped his gaze to her lips. He waited, almost expecting it to hurt his chest like when he had kissed Shubman. He waited long enough for Aditi to pull away with a strained smile.

"I… had some work with Rohit actually. I’ll see you later,” she said as she got up.

Ishan cursed under his breath before calling out, “Aditi!”

“Hm?”

“I like you.”

Aditi’s eyes widened slightly before she smiled. “I know.”

“Will you be my–”

Aditi chuckled as she gently pulled Ishan’s cheek, making him blush immediately. “First take me out on a proper date, Ish.”

“Right, of course.” Ishan couldn't contain the wide grin that spread across his face. "Would you like to go on a date with me tonight?”

“Thought you’d never ask.”

Shubman shouldn't have stepped away to get some air because if anything, he feltsuffocatedwatching Ishan’s confession.

He should be happy for his best friend, happy that Ishan finally got the girl he liked. But he wasn't. God, he really was a terrible human.

Shaking his thoughts off, he walked back to the group. Rishabh jumped on him like a madman. “Shastri was flirting with the Prof from Physiology! Can you believe it?”

Shubman tried to process the news, but there had been a lot of information in the span of an hour. He was about to call it a day and head back to the dorms to sleep off the rest of the day when Mayank asked him, “Did you find Ishan?”

“I wish I found him sooner.”

Title: CH: 16
Chapter_Text:
"Ish, let’s go for a movie,” Shubman said, though he knew the other’s reply.

Pausing from styling his hair, Ishan frowned at the unruly strands that kept falling on his forehead. "Sorry, meri jaan. I've got plans in an hour,” he said inattentively.

"Yeah, plans withme." Shubman grinned.

“No.” Ishan let out an amused huff, finally turning to him. "How about we go tomorrow?"

Shubman shot him an unimpressed look, his hand reaching out to gently brush Ishan’s hair aside. Ishan let out a small groan as more loose strands fell across his forehead.

“Are you meeting Aditi?”

“Yeah,” Ishan replied, a smile tugging at his lips.

“Is it a date?”

Warmth crept up Ishan’s ears as he felt a flutter of giddiness at the word date. “Yes, I’m so–”

“Don’t go.”

Ishan looked up at Shubman with a small frown. “What?”

Shubman inched closer, his hand gliding down Ishan's face before cradling his jaw. “I don't want you to go on a date with her.”

“Well,Iwant to.” Ishan tilted his head in confusion. “I’ll go to the movies with you tomorrow, alright?”

“What will you do on the date anyway?” Shubman asked in response.

Ishan averted his gaze from Shubman's face, which seemed to be drawing nearer with each passing minute. “You know… Um, just talk and…”

Shubman whispered, his breath warm against Ishan's skin, "Hold her hand?" He trailed his fingers down Ishan’s arm, brushing teasingly against his fingers before intertwining them.

“Y-Yeah.” Ishan glanced down at their locked fingers, wanting to break away but couldn't for some reason.

Shubman's gaze drifted down to Ishan's lips. "Kiss her?" he asked in a whisper, causing Ishan's breath to hitch slightly.

“I don't know,” he muttered weakly. Shubman’s eyes traced back to his eyes, intense and unwavering.

“I think you should practice a little, hm?”

“What are you talking about?” Ishan mumbled, licking his lips subconsciously.

Shubman smirked. “I know you’re not that docile, darling.”

Before Ishan could reply, Shubman's lips brushed against his own, lingering there, as if waiting for permission. He cussed mentally as he pressed forward, making Shubman smile against his lips.

Ishan moaned a little when Shubman bit on his lip while entering, followed by sucking on his tongue to compensate. Ishan’s smaller frame pressed against the other’s hard chest, their bodies flushing together. Shubman wrapped a hand around Ishan’s waist to pull him even closer.

"Can't let you go out when you taste likethis," Shubman whispered between rough little pants as they pulled apart for air.

“I… I need to go, Shub– ah.”

Ishan tilted his head back, inviting Shubman’s lips to his bare neck, where the boy left a trail of hot kisses. He tensed a little when Shubman’s hand reached for his ass. He waited for a grope but the hand slipped into his back pocket instead, retrieving his phone.

“Call her,” Shubman murmured as he held out the phone.

“What?” Ishan breathed heavily as Shubman returned to kissing his neck.

“It’s not nice to make a lady wait. Tell her you’ll be late,” he said with a knowing smirk.

“O-Okay,” Ishan replied shakily.

Aditi picked up on the third ring.“Hey, what's up?”

“Um, Adi. I’m so sorry I-”

Just then Shubman decided to lick a stripe across Ishan’s sweet spot, nearly causing Ishan to moan aloud.Fuck.

“Is something wrong, Ish?”

“Adi, I’ll be a little l-late. Is that f-fine?” he managed to say despite Shubman brutally sucking on his sweet spot.

“Of course, Ish. Text me when you’re ready.”

“Yeah. Thank you, Adi.”

“Take care,”she replied sweetly before the call ended.

Ishan was going to hell. Or maybe he was in hell. And the devil was tormenting him with sweet, dirty kisses.

“You’re such abad boy, Ish,” Shubman whispered.

“Shubi, this is wrong,” Ishan mumbled, biting his lip as the other continued marking his neck with little bruises.

“It’s not wrong if it feels right, meri jaan.”

Fuck Shubman and his manipulative voice. Ishan couldn't help but drape his hands around the other’s broad shoulders. “S-Stop…”

“Do you really want me to?” Shubman hummed.

No, Ishan’s growing boner screamed at him. He let out a sigh before glaring up at his friend, he then pulled him by his collar, bringing their mouths colliding again.

“You’ll take fucking responsibility for this, Gill.”

Shubman liked the ordering tone, not that he was going to admit it out loud. He just muttered a little “yessir” before pinning Ishan to the wall.

Ishan wasn't hesitant once the sensible part of his brain turned into mush. He ran his hands through Shubman's hair roughly, pulling him close, as their tongues swirled together. Shubman’s tongue slipped between his lips, tearing a blissful moan from him. Quickly, though, Shubman pulled back, breathing seemingly endless phrases out into Ishan’s ears, all the while kissing his jaw, his cheeks, his neck, until Ishan was helplessly leaning his head back against the wall.

"I've always been your first priority, Ish," Shubman whispered in his ear. "I don't like change."

"Y-Yes," Ishan almost whimpered.

Shubman bit Ishan’s neck harder than before, just above his collarbones. "Don’t youeverforget that," he snapped.

Ishan let out a sharp breath as he nodded, not knowing how else to react to his best friend’s possessiveness. “You know I’d never replace you, Shubi,” he mumbled.

“Replace?” Shubman sounded amused. “Jaan, you don't want to know the things I’d do to you if you eventhinkof replacing me.”

You'd think yourjust best friendspewing lines like that would maybe need a therapist on speed dial. But Ishan is a fucking idiot, so he doesn't. Fellas, don't let your love for your best friend distract you from their psychopathic tendencies.

“Sorry,” Ishan let out a sharp breath. “Fuck, Shubman, you got me hard.”

Shubman finally pulled back to admire the work of art beneath him. Beautiful. Ishan’s eyes were hooded, heavy with want. His lips were slightly swollen from all the kissing, and there was that hickey–Shubman’s hickey– peeking out from under his shirt.

He lowered his gaze to Ishan’s crotch, a sense of pride welling up within him. Despite how it felt like, he wasn't intoxicated, so he didn't reach out to touch Ishan’s dick. He didn't blur the last line between their friendship and lust.

“Will you just keep staring?” Ishan grumbled, his cheeks flushed red.

Shubman clicked his tongue, walking away. “You’ve got your little girlfriend to take care of it,” he said nonchalantly.

Ishan rolled his eyes, running his hand through his hair to fix it. “What the fuck were you being so adamant about kissing me then? Tch,” he muttered.

“Because,darling,” Shubman sighed. “When she kisses you tonight, you’ll know who does it better.”

Ishan’s mouth gaped a little before he threw a cushion at Shubman, who dodged it with a chuckle. “You narcissistic piece of shit!” Ishan fussed, shaking his head.

“Where are you going?” Shubman asked when Ishan headed towards the bathroom.

“To have a wank, motherfucker,” Ishan replied with a glare. “I can't walk around with a fucking boner, Can I?”

Shubman paused, a grin spreading across his face. He quickly stepped in between Ishan and the door, blocking the way. “Can?Darling, youwill.”

Ishan shot him a flat look. “What kind of weird porn have you been watching?”

Shubman shook his head innocently. “What if you got hard on the field? You can't have a wank then, can you? This is essentially a drill, Ishan. You must know how to control your body’s physiological reactions!”

“Don’t fuck with me right now,” Ishan whined. He tried to push the boy, but his muscles might as well have been made of bricks. Damn it.

“Shubman, seriously.”

“Iamserious, meri jaan.”

Ishan was so frustrated he might as well just jerk off right there. His brain (finally back to function) helpfully supplied that the chances of Shubman shamelessly taking his dick in his mouth were slim but never zero. So, he didn't want to risk it.

“Sick fucker, you just want to hear me begging, don’t you?” Ishan groaned.

Shubman smiled far too sweetly for the absolute evil he was being at the moment. “That's very tempting but I just want you to learn to have more control over your body, Ish.”

“Ah, fine!” Ishan said exasperatedly. He would just jerk off in the nearest public restroom. The sooner he got out of here, the better.

Shubman knew exactly what idea Ishan might have come up with. He decided to let it go, since he had teased the poor boy enough, but not before whispering one last time in his ear, “If you're a good boy until you come back to me, I’llrewardyou nicely.”

Ishan blushed furiously, feeling as if his face was on fire. He calmed down his thumping heart and walked away from the devil incarnate. “Fuck you and your reward!” he yelled before rushing out.

“Yeah, that was the idea!” Shubman retorted with a chuckle.

Ishan felt like he did a decent job not losing his mind in front of Aditi. He flashed her an apologetic smile, which she returned with a warm one of own.

They spent an hour talking comfortably, discussing everything from their upcoming matches to graduation. He might have brought up his best friend more times than one would on a date, but Aditi seemed to be a good listener. Ishan had sensed it from the moment he had started talking to her – they justclicked. Conversations with her didn't feel forced; he always felt at ease. The shy smiles and sneaky glances that they exchanged made Ishan feel like a middle schooler with a crush.

He loved the purity and innocence of their connection. Yet. Somehow there was aprimal,carnallonging that stirred within him when Shubman kissed him.

“Ishan?” Aditi asked, snapping him out of his thoughts.

"Sorry, what did you say?" Ishan nervously twirled his spoon in his coffee.

Aditi's gaze lingered on Ishan, her eyes narrowing slightly, instinctively drifting to the bruising mark down his neck.A hickey. Her fingers traced the edge of her dress as she looked away biting her lip.

“Nothing much,” she replied quietly. “Just that... you seem a little lost.”

“Oh, um. I just have some stuff on my mind right now. Sorry. I’m not being a very good date, am I?”

Aditi looked down with a small smile, though it appeared forced. “It’s okay, Ish. I had a good time today.”

Was it over already? Ishan wasn't sure if he was disappointed or relieved.

“Do you want to take a walk? There's still some time before curfew,” he suggested, not wanting to head back just yet forobvious reasons.

So, here they were, strolling hand-in-hand. Ishan wasn't sure who initiated it first; he just hoped his palm didn't get sweaty. A part of his brain also replayed the little moment back in his room with Shubman, which he promptly shoved away.

“You’re not seeing anyone, right?” Aditi asked suddenly.

Aditi's sudden question caught Ishan off guard for a moment. "I wouldn't be here if I were," he replied with a smile. “Why do you ask?”

Aditi hesitated a little before shaking her head. “I was just wondering how someone like you could be single,” she quipped with a grin.

Ishan chuckled, nudging her gently. “Saysyou.”

Aditi hummed, her eyes lifting to the moon's soft glow. "It's a beautiful tonight, the moon."

"Yeah," Ishan murmured, following her gaze to the sky. A smile tugged at his lips as he remembered something. "Back in the days, on summer nights, Shubman would stay over, and we would sneak out to the roof at midnight to stargaze.”

Aditi glanced at Ishan, waiting for him to look at her. She counted the seconds before finally planting a light kiss on Ishan’s cheek. He froze for a moment, his breath catching in his throat.

“A-Aditi?”

“Is it okay?” she whispered.

Ishan couldn't help but smile, his cheeks tinged with a blush. "Very," he replied softly. When he turned to lean in for a kiss, she gently placed her hand on his lips, causing him to pout in playful protest.

“Not so fast, hero,” she said with a chuckle, removing her hand.

Ishan sighed, nodding. “Come on, I’ll walk you back to your dorm.”

Aditi wished she were braver, to ask about the hickey, about the way Ishan's eyes gleamed when he mentioned Shubman, just like they did when they admired the moon together. But she was, after all, just a girl who liked a boy too much.

Ishan decided to steer clear of his room, not eager to spend a night with a possibly horny Shubman. Instead, he headed to the gym down the block. He frowned at the unusual buzz of the equipment, wondering who would be working out at this hour.

“Your posture is off,” Ishan called out, his voice reverberating in the otherwise empty gym.

Yashasvi dropped the weights with a grunt, casting a quick glance at the opponent skipper. He puffed his chest, going for another try when Ishan swatted at his arm.

“God, you’re drenched! How long have you been at this?” Ishan asked, noticing Yashasvi's sweat-soaked shirt.

Yashasvi mumbled, his cheeks flushing. “None of your business.”

“Still mad you got stumped at zero, huh?” Ishan grinned.

“Please don’t bother me,” Yashasvi said, though he allowed Ishan to help him. “Thanks,” he muttered.

"You're pretty dedicated," Ishan remarked, watching him. "Meanwhile, your captain is sleeping like a log.”

Yashasvi dropped the weights, finally catching a breath. “He’s always too casual after losing matches. I don't like that about him.”

Ishan chuckled, tossing a towel at the boy. "Every captain has his own style. But hey, he's still good, right?”

Yashasvi remained quiet for a moment before shrugging. “We still lost, it doesn't matter.”

“You’ve won two consecutive times,” Ishan pointed out.

Yashasvi clenched his fists, his expression turning serious. “That just means we had to win for the third time too.”

Ishan crossed his arms, staring at the other. “You remind me of myself.”

“Is that supposed to be a compliment?” Yashasvi snorted.

Ishan shrugged, walking away. He suddenly remembered Shubman talking about this Yashasvi guy.“Has a resting bitch face but a good lad,”he had said. Ishan figured he should mind his own business and leave his opponents be. Even though his brain starts to tingle when he can’t befriend everyone within a mile radius, he knew he should respect people's boundaries.

But he had an idea what Yashasvi was going through. Overworking to punish himself, that wasn't something Ishan was unfamiliar with.

“Aren’t you gonna work out?” Yashasvi called after him.

Ishan glanced over his shoulder with a smirk. “Race you to the mess,” he challenged, taking off.

“Wha- Oi!” Yashasvi cursed at thestrangeboy who thought he would follow him.As if.

Yashasvi caught up with Ishan in no time.

“You’re fast!” Ishan laughed as they raced down the empty hallway. “Not bad, Yashu.”

“Don’t call me that,” Yashasvi panted, but a small smile tugged at his lips as he pushed himself forward.

“I guess you won,” Ishan mused as they reached the mess hall, slowing down. He patted the other’s back brightly, causing Yashasvi to scoff.

“Yeah, where's mytrophy?”

“In the pantry,” Ishan replied with a mischievous grin, already sneaking around the corner for another one of his pantry raids.

Yashasvi shifted on his feet uneasily. “Do you wanna get suspended?” he hissed, glancing around for cameras.

Ishan gasped. “Rishabh beat me to it!”

Only Rishabh could be faster than Ishan in these raids. They had been partners in crime many times before, but this time, Rishabh decided to be abetrayer.

“I think we should get out of here,” Yashasvi muttered, hearing footsteps in the distance.

“Can you believe Rishabh took my stock of Mogu Mogu?” Ishan turned to him, pouting.

Yashasvi grabbed his arm. “Seriously, you are so strange.”

Somehow, Ishan led them towards Rishabh’s room. “Listen here, you little tsundere, we are going to reclaim our rightful Mogu Mogu!”

“Rightful? You mean rightfullystolen?”

“I don't care about semantics,” Ishan muttered, as he attempted to quietly turn the doorknob.

“This is so creepy,” Yashasvi mumbled. What was he even doing here, being an accomplice toMeridian'sbiggest rival captain in some cheap, midnight tomfoolery? He was going to get caught and bring shame to his uni. TheseSummitbrats could afford to waste their time like this, but Yashasvi couldn't.

As Ishan pushed the door open, Yashasvi gaped at the scene. “Dhruv?!”

“Oh, Yashu! Check this out, they’ve got the Passion Fruit flavor,” Dhruv beamed, showing off the two packs he had managed to get, most probably for Yashasvi.

A very pleased Rishabh, sitting next to him, smirked at Ishan. Ishan gasped as he watched Rishabh down the last pack of Mango flavor.

Yashasvi shook his head with a sigh. “Let's go, Dhruv. We have to leave early tomorrow.”

“What a party pooper,” Ishan scoffed. “Let the boy have his dose of happiness.”

Yashasvi ignored him, focusing on Dhruv. “You know, your disciplinary points would have been cut off if you pulled this atMeridian.”

Rishabh barked a laugh at that. “Ish, they have a disciplinary point system! What are you, kindergarteners?”

“Geez, what a bunch of stuck-ups,” Ishan chuckled, picking up a pack for himself.

Dhruv’s shoulders slumped a little as his grip tightened around the drink. “We’ll have to live in that hellhole from tomorrow anyway, can't you let me be for once?” he mumbled.

Yashasvi stared at him. “Hell- what? Dhruv, do you realize how that sounds? You want to talk like that about ourhomein front of them?”

“Kya galat bola usne?” Rishabh interjected, sensing Dhruv’s discomfort. “It's no secret the torture your so-calledeliteuni puts their recruits through.”

“Stop brainwashing him,” Yashasvi snapped. “That is the cost of winning. If you don't give, you can't take–”

“I did give my best!” Dhruv protested, his voice cracking slightly. “And we still lost.”

“That's because I played like shit,” Yashasvi muttered, clenching his fists.

“I’m already sad, Yashasvi. Don't make me sadder,” Dhruv replied.

Yashasvi winced at the use of his full name. “Okay, I’ll go.”

Ishan sighed and smacked Yashasvi on the back of his head. “Go and sit down.”

“I’m not taking orders from you.”

Ishan shot him a flat look. “Your body is dehydrated after all that working out. Have a fucking drink before I drag your sorry ass to the third years and leave you there for a wonderful night of ragging.”

Yashasvi blinked. “You guys are so weird.”

Ishan shoved him onto one of the beds before tossing a pack to him. “I shouldn't be doing this,” he mumbled to himself, looking down at the drink in his hand.

“Live a little, will you?” Ishan said as he sat beside the boy.

“What would you have done if you lost?” Yashasvi asked pointedly.

Ishan grinned. “Raided thewholepantry.”

“Is it a crime to be serious here?” Yashasvi whined.

Rishabh chuckled. “Maybe you should rephrase the question. Ish, what would theoldyou have done if we lost?”

Ishan sighed, rubbing his neck. “I would have practiced until I passed out, I guess.”

Yashasvi sparked up at that. “So, why do you stop me?”

“Because you are not a robot, Yashasvi. You are a human and a human deserves rest. Whether you win or you lose, sometimes all you can do is let it go.”

Yashasvi spoke after a pause, “What after that?”

"After that... you practice until your limbs fall off, silly!" Ishan laughed, smacking him again. "But not now. Not when you've just lost and you feel like the worst person alive.”

Yashasvi blinked. “Alright,” he muttered in a small voice. He didn't know how else to respond to just being ripped open apart by someone who didn't evenknowhim.

Ishan grinned, tousling Yashasvi's sweaty hair before recoiling. “Ugh, gross! You need a shower. Right now.”

“He’s a stinkball,” Dhruv chuckled. “He always works out at night and crashes just like that.”

“Let's throw him into the fountain tomorrow,” Rishabh quipped happily.

“Why, you’re a genius, Rishu!” Ishan giggled. Yashasvi rolled his eyes, silently sipping his drink.

“They are fun, aren't they?” Dhruv nudged him with a wide grin.

“We are the most fun!” Ishan exclaimed, grinning as well.

Yashasvi looked at both the boys, beaming like idiots. “Too bright, I’ll go fucking blind,” he grunted to himself.

The two sunshine boys exchanged a confused glance, while Rishabh chuckled, nodding his head. “I know, right?”

What anoddbunch of people. He wished he could play against them once again. Too bad they were graduating in a few months.

Shubman was pissed. Ishan had stayed out the whole night. Wasn't it just a date? Did they sleep together? Couldn't Ishan have at least informed him? Shubman had even built a pillow fort for them to watch Star Wars together. He ended up getting smacked by Rohit for stealing his pillows because Shubman needed more.

He had planned to leave later in the day, but when Ishan didn't show up by morning, he packed his bags and joined his teammates. He felt petty enough to drop a few texts.

Shubiseriously?the least you could dowas see me off.im so fucking mad at you ishan.don't you dare call me.

Ishan read the texts from the notification bar after slipping into his room once Shubman had left. He sighed upon seeing the messy attempt at making a pillow fort. Next to it was an untouched parcel of cake where it was written,"Congratulations for winning!"

Ishan did feel a little guilty, but he was nervous to face Shubman for some reason. He checked the time, cursing quietly when he realized he had planned to meet Aditi outside her dorm.

He could just call Shubman later.

“He still hasn't called!” Shubman exclaimed, pacing around in his room.

Yashasvi nodded inattentively as he sipped on his last pack of his drink. “What did you tell him?”

“To not call me.”

Yashasvi snorted. “You, my brother, are the problem.”

Shubman frowned at his junior. “You’re taking his side? Don't you hate thoseSummitbrats?”

“Don't you hate them too?”

“Well, Ishan is my best friend.”

“He’s my friend too,” Yashasvi muttered.

Shubman raised an eyebrow. “Since when?”

“Yesterday, we bonded over this,” Yashasvi said, pointing at the drink.

“But he was with me until… Wait, when’s this about?” Shubman clenched his fists.

“At night, in Rishabh bhai’s room. I think he crashed there–”

Shubman didn't wait for him to finish before he rushed out,fuming. So, Ishanwasavoiding him. He pulled out his phone to call him.

“What happened?”Ishan asked nonchalantly.

“You tell me,” Shubman snarked. “What is up withyou?”

“Can we talk later? I’m kind of busy.”

“You’re with her again, aren't you?”

“Yeah, so?”

“Nothing.” Shubman pinched the bridge of his nose in agitation. “You are a dickhead, Ishan.”

“What- Okay, whatever. I’ll call you back. Bye.”

Shubman felt like smashing his phone on the wall as Ishan hung up on him but it cost him a fortune so he didn't. Instead, he screamed like an idiot as he ran around in circles.

“What’s up with Cap?” Sai asked, turning to Dhruv.

“The loss has finally settled in for him, I guess,” Dhruv said with a shrug. “You wanna try some Mogu Mogu? I stole it fromSummit.”

“Sure.”

Aditi chuckled as Ishan carefully placed petals in her hair. “What are you doing, Ish?”

“Showing the way to honeybees.”

“That’s so sweet,” Aditi cooed. “But they might hurt me, you know.”

Ishan blinked, sighing. “Sorry, I’m not very good at flirting.”

“Are you flirting with me?” she asked, wiggling her eyebrows.

"Haven't I made it clear?" Ishan retorted, copying her action and placing a flower in his own hair.

"I guess you’ve not," Aditi said with a feigned pout.

"Should I make it clear?" Ishan teased, leaning in a little. He chuckled when she lightly slapped his cheek.

"You’re not sleek, Ishu Kishu."

Ishan scrunched his nose at the name. "Ugh, that's so—"

“Cute!”

“No!”

“It's cute when I say it,” Aditi said, looking satisfied.

Ishan narrowed his eyes before whispering, “Okay, you got me there.”

They both giggled and continued their soft whispers until she asked a question that took him by surprise. “Have you heard the rumour about Shubman?”

“What rumour?”

Ishan tilted his head in confusion. Shubman always seemed to have some sort of rumor swirling around him, but Ishan never paid much attention to them. Until this one.

“Some people say he’s… gay.”

Ishan felt like he had been punched in the gut. There was no way the word could have gotten out unless Shubman had told someone else, or worse, hooked up with a boy. His hand instinctively rose to fiddle with his collar, trailing over the fading hickey.

“Don't believe everything people say,” he replied, trying to sound casual.

“I won’t.” Aditi said, staring at him. “But should I believeyou?”

Ishan looked at her, panic creeping into his eyes. “W-What do you mean?”

“Ish, are you hiding something from me?” Aditi asked, her tone serious. “Or should I say– from yourself?”
