Title: CH: 18
Chapter_Text:
“Why would I hide anything from you?” Ishan said with a weak smile.

“Okay,” Aditi replied before starting to get up.

Ishan panicked, grabbing her hand. “No, please, Adi. If you're worried about something, talk to me.”

Aditi looked at him, her expression softening a bit. “I’m not worried if you aren't hiding anything.”

Ishan rubbed his palms together soothingly, his eyes searching Aditi's face for any sign of hesitation. “Let me walk you to class?”

Aditi’s eyes darted away for a moment before she shook her head slowly. “I actually have to meet a friend, so–”

“Please don't lie to me,” Ishan muttered, his voice tinged with a hint of desperation.

Aditi gulped, her fingers fidgeting with the strap of her bag. “Aren’t you?”

Ishan took a deep breath, his shoulders slumping as he dropped his head onto her shoulder, his grip on her hand tightening slightly. “I’m sorry.”

Aditi rubbed his back, trying to offer what little comfort she could, even though her heart felt heavy. “Are you seeing someone else? Is that it?”

Ishan blinked, pulling back. “What- No! Why would you think that?”

Aditi shot him a confused frown. “Well, you know,the hickey.”

Ishan fixed his collar instinctively, sighing. “It’s not what you think. This is not– This isn't from a girl.”

“Huh?”

Ishan’s cheeks flushed in embarrassment as he muttered, “It's from Shubman.”

Aditi stared at him. “What?”

For some reason, she wasn't surprised. Then she was worried that she wasn't surprised. “Excuse me?” she asked.

“It’s a little complicated,” Ishan grunted.

Aditi’s eyes widened as she stepped back a little. “Is the rumor true? You–”

“No!” Ishan exclaimed, his voice laced with panic. “He's not gay. Please don’t tell anyone, he has such a bright future. He’s not anything of that sort, so please–”

“Hey, it’s okay,” Aditi reassured him, placing a hand on his shoulder. “I’m not telling anyone. Ish, it’sokayif he is.”

Ishan’s eyes flickered with panic. “No, he's not!”

“Okay, okay. He's not, I get it.” Aditi sighed, pulling him into a hug.

‘Oh, he definitely is’,her brain quipped.

She gulped, deciding if she should ask again. “But I still need to know what happened between you two, Ish.”

Ishan hesitated, slowly drawing away. Seeing his discomfort, she said, “I would never judge you, Ish. But I deserve to know.”

Ishan sighed, running a hand through his hair nervously. “I did... something stupid with Shubman. It was just a one-time thing… that, um, might have happened afewtimes.”

‘Oh, Aditi, we’re really in it now,’her brain quipped once again.

Aditi blinked, trying to process what he was saying. “Is this a... boys thing?” she managed to speak.

Ishan looked away, biting his lip. “I don't think so. But! It didn't mean anything, I swear.”

“Wow, um.” Aditi looked up at the summer sky, trying to stay calm. “I don't know what to say, Ish.”

What did she get herself into? Was this cheating? But they weren't together. Were Ishan and Shubman together? But it was very unlikely. Should she dump iced coffee on Ishan? But that was a hassle. Should she get a therapist? Probably.

Aditi sighed, running her fingers through her hair. “You know, I can't even decide if this counts as cheating because we aren't even together. What are we, Ish?”

Ishan’s eyes widened, a mix of panic and guilt flashing across his face. “Okay, I’m sorry. I really am sorry. Please, just don’t leave me,” he pleaded.

“Huh? Why wouldIleave?” Aditi quirked an eyebrow. “Do you not like girls, Ish?”

“What? Obviously I do. You’ve seen me date–”

“Don't say her name!”

“Oh, okay. Wait, I mean, why are you asking me that?!”

“Listen here, you little piece of sunshine, I’m not just going to simply hand you over to that pole,” Aditi said, poking his chest.

“What are you even talking about–”

“Tell him to fight for you if he has the balls,” Aditi declared, crossing her arms.

“I’m so confused,” Ishan mumbled. “Adi– Wait, where are you going?!”

“To get prettier!” Aditi stomped off, leaving him to have an existential crisis in the middle of the park. “His lashes are longer than mine. Ugh!”

Ishan blinked. God, he had ruined a perfectly sane girl.

“Why haven't you been picking up my calls?”

Ishan could imagine Shubman’s puffed cheeks on the other side of the call. He added, “Kya hua, meri jaan?”

“Don't call me that!”

Oh, no. He’s pissed. Ishan sighed, running his hand through his hair as he flopped on his bed. “Thank you for the cake, Shubman.”

“Don't call me that either!”

“Okay, fine. If you don't want to talk to me then goodbye.” Ishan chuckled at the whine that followed.

“You totally ghosted me yesterday,”Shubman said, probably pouting.

“I returned in the morning.”

“Yeah, after I left.”

“I’m sorry, Shubi. I was hanging out with Rishabh and I was too tired to walk back to the room.”

“Okay, but you could have come to see me off.”

“I woke up late, Shubi.”

“Stop giving excuses. I know you were avoiding me.”

Ishan bit the inside of his cheek. “Why would I ever avoid you?”

“So we're ignoring the three years in between.”

“Oh, come on,” Ishan groaned. “Anyway, I got in trouble because of your stupid hickey. Aditi thought we were something.”

“Huh, what thing?”

“I don't know, I didn't quite get what she was saying. Girls are a little hard to understand.”

“Yeah, don't say,”Shubman snorted.

Ishan giggled before he gasped. “God, do you know there are rumours about you being gay?”

Shubman went silent before sighing.“Yeah, I know.”

“What did you do, Shubi?”

“What do you think I’d do?”

“You tell me, did you kiss a boy?”

“Are you jealous?”Shubman teased.

Ishan scoffed, “I’m jealous, yeah, of your audacity to crack jokes in the middle of a crisis.”

“I’ve lived in this crisis for years. It’s alright, Ish.”

Ishan hummed, rolling on his back to stare at the ceiling. “Shouldn’t we do something to stop the rumour?”

“Believe me, they just need someone to blame the loss on. What's a better scapegoat than an alleged gay Captain?”

“Are people mean to you, Shubi?” Ishan asked softly.

Shubman had always been a gentle soul in school. He used to be a timid, shy boy, but once he opened up, there was no shutting him up. His voice was softer than most other boys, tagged asfeminineby some. He just had a late puberty, alright?

Shubman might have changed himself, embracing narcissism and wearing a fearless smile, but that soft boy from school never disappeared; he just learned to hide better. The child in him never grew, only his body did.

Shubman paused before chuckling.“I can't call the world mean just because no one cares for me the way you did.”

Ishan smiled to himself, snuggling into his pillow. “What do you mean ‘did’? I still do.”

“Do you?”

Ishan frowned at the hesitant tone, sitting up. “Of course.”

“Liar! You don’t ghost people you care about.”

“We’re back to square one,” Ishan groaned. “Okay, I made aghastly betrayal.How shall I compensate, meri jaan?”

Shubman giggled, followed by a satisfied hum.“Give me a kiss,”he said, making exaggerated smooching noises.

“Grown ass man.”

“Just tell me if you want me dead, Ish.”Shubman sniffled dramatically.

“Talk to you later, bro.”

“Rude much,”Shubman grumbled, stopping his act.“If I don't get a kiss now, you know what I can do to get it.”

Ishan’s heart did a little flip every time Shubman joked about it. Couldn't the other just have some respect for his sanity?

“Um, Rohit’s yelling my name.”

“Bach gaya saala. You better hurry then. Bye, jaan.”

“Bye.”

Ishan fell back on the bed with a sigh. Aditi’s question echoed in his mind– “What are we?”

He rubbed his temples with a small groan before letting his hands fall by his sides. The same question burned on his lips, wanting to be asked to Shubman.

The next time they met each other was after two weeks, at the club. Ishan was nursing a drink at the bar when someone caged him from behind; someone who scented of lavender even in the pool of sweaty junkies.

Shubman grinned as he leaned on the bar counter. "Does your little girlfriend know you’re out having fun alone?”

“Mylittle girlfriend’sright here,” Ishan said, pointing vaguely.

Rishabh had dragged the team to check out the new club. Ishan had thought he should have the balls to ask Aditi out, so he did. He's glad he did. He could now rely on her to distract him from staring at Shubman’s teasingly revealed chest.

God, he was already tipsy. Ishan tried to excuse himself, afraid Shubman would make him do something stupid again. Though their last time at club was veryeventful, Ishan was sure his girlfriend-not-girlfried wouldn't approve of him exchanging spit with his best friend.

Shubman followed him, nonetheless. He flashed his sweetest smile at Aditi, who returned it with an equally sweet one. "Leave," they both thought at the same time.

“Why, you lookgorgeousas ever,” Shubman said, his voice laced with honey.

“Not more than you,” Aditi replied. “Wouldn't you agree,Ishan?”

Ishan felt uneasy as both of them turned their attention to him, waiting for a response. How did he always end up in such situations? He took a swig of his drink and replied, “Uhhh. I look better than both of you actually.”

Shubman and Aditi blinked, exchanging a confused glance before slowly nodding.

“Oh, ho, ho. Look who’s here,” Surya called out, slapping Shubman’s back.

“Sex on legs just walked in,” Mayank slurred, leaning against an irritated Abhishek. “Leave some girls for us, bro!”

“Gill’s scoring tonight. Who wants to bet?” Hardik chimed in with a grin.

Shubman laughed, running a hand through his hair. Hewasfeeling himself tonight, especially with the glances that Ishan kept stealing at him. Noticing the verytargetedsmirk on the boy’s face, Ishan rolled his eyes. He engaged in light chatter with the others, avoiding both Shubman and Aditi’s stares. Especially when hisexwalked up to him.

They barely dated for three months before mutually deciding that she was too hot for him. Anyway, there wasn't any bad blood between them, so he didn't read too much into her touches that lingered on his arm. Unlike two people.

“Sly bitch,”they both muttered at the same time.

Aditi looked at Shubman, surprised. “That’s rude,” she said.

Shubman cleared his throat, averting his gaze. “Don’t be a hypocrite.”

“I’m his girl– Well, we’retogether. I can be rude about his ex hitting on him,” Aditi said, raising an eyebrow.

“Hisex?” Shubman hissed, diverting Aditi’s next question. “He had a girlfriend?”

Aditi scrunched her nose, nodding. “In second year. I don't like her.”

Shubman looked back at the girl, her hand still on Ishan’s arm. “Yeah, me neither.”

Aditi raised her glass towards him. “Shubman, can I ask you something?”

“No.”

“Fuck you then.”

“Cheers,” Shubman grinned, clinking their glasses together.

“What was her name again,”Ishan thought to himself as he tried to focus on whatever his ex was saying.

“Why did you drink so much if you have such a low tolerance?” Abhishek whined, trying to push away Mayank, who was clinging to him like a koala.

“Aren't you here to take care of me?” Mayank giggled, rubbing his cheek against Abhishek's, causing the other to wince at the stubble.

“No, you ugly monkey.”

Mayank scowled, throwing a weak punch at the other. “How dare you call me ugly?! You–! You’re–! Damn, I can't even call you ugly.”

Abhishek shifted as Mayank's face drew a little too close for his liking. "Why?" he mumbled.

Mayank blinked, then turned to face Abhishek, cupping his face rather aggressively, sandwiching it between his hands. “You’re so cute! Cutie patatooie, kuchi kuchi–”

“Fuck off,” Abhishek finally shoved him, causing him to land on the sofa with a groan.

“You’re not half bad either,” he added with an amused smile.

Title: CH: 19
Chapter_Text:
Shubman was pathetically bored. No, that did not have anything to do with the lack of Ishan's attention he was getting at the moment.

Ishan, who was dancing with Aditi, nervously tucked his hand around her waist as they both shyly glanced around. Shubman rolled his eyes at the sight. At this point, Ishan had gone further with Shubman than he had with hisgirlfriend.

You see, Shubman didn't mind his best friendgetting some. He just had a distaste for having to watch it play out right in front of him. His grip on his glass grew tighter as he swallowed an ugly feeling. Whether Ishan loved her or not didn't matter; when he was in the room, Shubman wanted Ishan's eyes on him alone. He demanded every ounce of Ishan’s attention like a rotten dog, baring his teeth in open defiance.

Ishan flashed him a small smile when their eyes met across the dance floor. Shubman took an angry swig of his drink, scoffing to himself. What was he smiling about? What was there to smile about when he could love a girl at his disposal, but Shubman couldn't? Not a girl, never a boy. No one.

There were times when Shubman felt like he wasn't built to surviveanyhuman relationship. The very notion of him feeling like he had tosurvivewithin one was the reason. Maybe that's why he denied any feelings that rose like bile in his throat. If he were ever to love someone, he would love like a beast, like a monster, like something not worth loving back.

So he searched, his eyes flickering under the neon lights, for someone who would see him for the monster he was. He smirked when he caught a guy staring at him, unashamedly so.How convenient. Shubman strode up to him, giving the other a chance to step back. He didn't.

"Wanna take a breather?" Shubman asked with a grin, gesturing vaguely towards the restroom.

The guy, probably younger than him, nodded hesitantly. It was easy to communicate when you both came from the same rot. Shubman glanced at Ishan over his shoulder as they made their way. Ishan's eyes held a confused glint, watching them intently. The satisfaction was small but tangible.

They weren't here for small talk, and they both knew it. So, the kissing was more like an attack, aprimalinstinct taking over. Shubman wasn't sure if he even properly saw the boy's face under the dim lights. But it didn't matter. All he cared about was the heat of the moment, the messy collision of teeth and tongue mixed with the alcohol enough to drive Shubman back into his narcissism, where he held the power.

Shubman closed his eyes, realising the sensation of lips and teeth on his neck. He tilted his head back, giving the boy better access. “Don’t leave marks,” he muttered.

The boy nodded, peppering kisses instead. Shubman gripped his hair, tugging it slightly to bring him down. “I didn't ask you to be gentle,” he grunted.

The boy nodded once again, his fingers hovering over Shubman’s waistband when the loud creak of the door startled him. Shubman didn't open his eyes, even when he felt the other boy scramble to his feet and hurry out of the restroom.

He imagined the expression on Ishan’s face. Would he be angry? Hurt? Betrayed?

“Took you long enough, Ish.”

He knew he was right when he finally opened his eyes. The only mistake— it wasn't Ishan.

Shubman’s heart dropped, and he could barely whisper, “Abhi?”

"What were you doing, Shubman?" Abhishek's voice sounded cold, breaking the heavy silence between them.

“I can explain,” Shubman managed, feeling his throat go dry.

“Yeah, you better.”

Shubman felt tears welling up in his eyes. Abhishek had never looked so calm before, it was not a good sign. "Can you at least come closer to me?" he asked, his voice tinged with pleading.

Abhishek clenched his jaw, his feet rooted to the spot. “Shubman,don’t.”

“I…I was just fooling around. I don't even know his name, I swear,” Shubman’s voice trembled.

“You call thatfooling around?” Abhishek snapped. “Do you know what you just proved?”

Shubman stepped forward slowly, afraid Abhishek would be disgusted by him.

“The rumours– Abhi, they are–” Shubman started, his voice barely a whisper.

“They are fucking true, aren't they?” Abhishek interrupted, his tone cutting through the air like a knife.

Shubman's eyes welled up with tears, he shook his head. “No, I– I would never do that to you. Abhi, please, listen to me.”

“When have I ever not listened to you, Shubman?” Abhishek's voice turned steely again. “Ever since we first met, I’ve listened to your dreams about becoming the best cricketer in the country. I’ve listened when you talked about Star Wars even though I couldn't care less about it. I’ve listened to you cry like a child every night after Ishan left.”

“W-Why are you being like this,” Shubman sniffled.

“Why? Because Ishan knew about it, didn't he?”

“About what–”

“You knowwhat, Shubman.”

When he didn't answer, Abhishek asked, “How long has Ishan known about it?”

“Abhi–”

“Just. Answer. Me.”

“Since tenth grade,” he whispered.

Abhishek took a sharp breath before mumbling, “I see. I’m just a sidekick, after all.”

“What the fuck are you on about?” Shubman reached out to hold his hand, but Abhishek flinched away.

“Shubman, please. I have had enough of youandIshan.”

Shubman looked at him in disbelief before a surge of anger took over. Without thinking, his fist shot up, gripping Abhishek by his collar and pinning him to the wall. “Take that back before I break your fucking nose.”

“Why? You thought you and Ishan are the only ones who could throw tantrums?” Abhishek sneered, his voice thick with bitterness.

“What'swrongwith you?” Shubman whispered, pulling away, hurt evident in his eyes.

Abhishek’s eyes softened for a moment. “Ifyoustill can't see what's wrong, then there’s no point in talking,” he said, stepping back.

Shubman cursed under his breath, shoving the other into the wall once again. "For fuck’s sake, Abhi," Shubman spat out, his voice strained with frustration. "I'm gay. Is that what you wanted to hear?”

“No, it's not.” Abhishek looked away, he seemed close to tears as well. “Notnow.”

“You’ve never been this difficult,” Shubman sighed exasperatedly.

“Difficult, huh?” Abhishek mumbled bitterly. “Yeah, I am. I’m every bit as difficult, sensitive, andhumanas you both are. Just because I don't shout, just because I don'tleave, just because I’myour Abhi, doesn't mean I don't get hurt.”

Shubman’s eyes widened, and he tried to pull Abhishek into a hug, but the other declined. “I didn't mean it like that, Abhi, I’m sorry. Please tell me what's hurting you.Please.”

“What else can hurt me like this but both of my best friends?” Abhishek whispered, a sad smile playing on his lips. “Who else but you two hold such power over me that I stay despite knowing you don't need me?”

“We do need you, Abhi!” Shubman shook him by the shoulder, his voice filled with desperation. “We love you so much.”

“Not in the way you need each other,” Abhishek muttered bitterly. “That's fine. Something's always been too special between you and Ishan. You two hurt each other in a way only you can mend each other. But did it ever occur to you that seeing you two hurt, hurt me too? Ever wondered whose shoulder I cried on while I was busy lending mine to yours? Did you ever ask yourself if I was only a replacement for Ishan when he wasn't around you?”

Shubman's breath caught in his throat, his heart sinking at Abhishek's words. “I would never do that. Abhi, I love you. Please–”

“You wanted tomoldme into Ishan in those three years, Shubman,” Abhishek said, his eyes locked on the other’s. “You made me wear your shirts, you slept in my bed, you held my hand, you made me watch Star Wars until I had nightmares of fucking Anakin Skywalker. That's just not us, Shubman. We don't do that. You stopped going to the arcade with me, you stopped ordering my favorite pizza without me having to tell you, you stopped talking to me about your dreams of joining the national team. These may seem like small things to you, but not to me.”

With every passing second, Shubman found it harder to breathe. Before he knew it, he was crying.

“And, Ish?” Abhishek added. “He didn't even look at me when he leftthatday. He didn’t even consider that leaving you meant leaving me too. I wasn’t even worth a fight. None of you two realize that I was seventeen too; it'smyfirst time living too, Shubman.”

“I’m sorry, I’m sorry, I’m sorry,” Shubman repeated like a prayer, one that would not be heard this time.

“I’m not upset that you didn't tell me,” Abhishek spoke. “My heart breaks because you didn't trust me after all these yearsIhave tried to mold myself for you, Shubman.”

Shubman shook his head helplessly as his tears blurred his vision. “I trust you with my life, Abhi. I will never hide anything from you again,I promise. Please, don't be mad at me.”

Abhishek felt a lump form at the back of his throat as he heard the crack in Shubman's voice. He willed Shubman's hands away, unable to meet the gaze of the face he had loved like his own.

Abhishek stared at his best friend, daring him to stop him. He didn't.

“I’m not going todilutemyself anymore, you canchoke,” Abhishek said before closing the door behind him.

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Title: CH: 20
Chapter_Text:
Ishan found Shubman on the pavement outside the club, taking slow, deliberate drags of his cigarette. He was about to give Shubman a piece of his mind for disappearing without a word, but his words caught in his throat when he noticed tears streaking down the other’s face.

“You smoke?” Ishan asked, cautiously standing next to him.

Shubman shook his head, still in a daze. “Just the third time,” he mumbled.

“When did you start?”

Shubman paused to let out a wisp. “After I saw you for the first time in eight months,” he said.

Ishan didn't respond, a knot of guilt tightening in his chest. He remembered ignoring the boy back then, as if he wasn't dying to see him.

Shubman added, “Next was after I kissed a boy for the first time."

He turned to face Ishan, andGod, did he look like a mess.A beautiful mess. Ishan cleared his throat, asking, “What’s the reason today?”

All evening, Shubman had been acting...off. Ishan couldn't quite put his finger on it, but his friend seemed on edge. And then, that happened. Shubman walked out with some guy, and Ishan couldn't help feeling a little uneasy about it.

When Shubman didn't answer, Ishan pressed, “Who was that guy from earlier?”

Shubman shot him a half-hearted smirk; he would have teased Ishan if he wasn't feeling like shit. “I don't know,” he muttered.

"You... and him... you know?" Ishan asked, making vague suggestive gestures with his hands, causing Shubman to scoff.

“You can saykiss, Ishan.”

Ishan’s cheeks flared up in embarrassment. “Right. So–”

“Abhi saw it.”

Ishan’s eyes widened, his mind racing. “Did he say something stupid, I swear–”

“No,” Shubman mumbled. “I’m the stupid one.”

“What happened, Shubi?” Ishan asked softly, his hand gently cupping Shubman’s cheek as he looked into his eyes with concern.

“What if he never talks to me, Ish? I’m not used to living without him. God, he hates me, doesn't he?” Shubman rambled, sniffling in between.

Ishan blinked, wondering what could have possibly happened between the two. But his heart ached seeing Shubman close to tears. Ishan inhaled sharply, diving for his bike keys as he grabbed Shubman's hand and tugged him along.

“W-What are you doing?”

“Why is always one of us running away? “ Ishan grumbled. “I’m not letting you two act like either of you are fine without each other. I need to hear what Abhi has to say since you won't do the honour.”

Shubman looked down, not responding, as he let Ishan steer him along. “I’m sorry, Ish.”

“…”

“Ishan.”

“I think you're saying it to the wrong person.”

Abhishek glared at the framed photo of the three of them in his room, taken on the night before their U-17 final. They had big smiles on their faces, clearly holding in laughter because Ishan probably cracked a joke. He wondered if that was the last time they weretogether. They were still together, but sometimes ‘together’ meant three summers ago, stumbling over tomato gardens and racing past mahoganies.

The final was a turning point in their lives that they couldn't try and undo. Abhishek wanted to at least redo it. Then he would have noticed Ishan slipping into his self-sabotaging habits. Then he would have noticed Shubman’s fidgeting whenever they talked about girls. Then he would have noticed how lonelyhefelt.

Abhishek nearly fell off his bed when the door to his room burst open. “Ish, what the fuck?”

Ishan fell silent, turning his attention to someone behind the door. “Stop hiding and come out right now,” he spoke to the person.

“I am gay–”

“Not that one!”

“Sorry, I’m nervous!”

Abhishek blinked in surprise as a slumped Shubman slowly walked in, his eyes glued to the floor. After a minute of shifting on his feet, he was shoved onto the bed by an irritated Ishan.

Abhishek looked away as Shubman toppled onto the bed next to him. “What do you want?” he asked, turning to Ishan.

“We want to apologize.”

“For what?”

“For being terrible friends?” Ishan offered, after trying to come up with a generalized answer.

Abhishek stared at him before scoffing. “You don't even know what happened.”

Ishan sighed, crouching down next to Abhishek. “I know that you're upset about something.”

“Upset?” Abhishek’s voice came out in a whisper. “I’m hurt, Ishan. I’m in pain and I don't know how to stop it.”

Ishan softened at how fragile Abhishek looked. He slowly reached out to cup Abhishek’s face, gently cradling it. “I’m sorry. I love you. Tell me what happened, please?”

Abhishek fought against crumbling down to his emotions as he pushed Ishan away slowly. “Why don't you ask yourbest friend?” he muttered, causing Shubman to wince.

“I am, aren't I?” Ishan asked, tilting his head in confusion. Abhishek clenched his jaw, remaining silent. Ishan clicked his tongue in realization. “Oh, that's what this is about.”

Abhishek kept staring at the wall, pretending the other two didn't exist.

“It’s not just about today, meri jaan, is it?” Ishan asked softly. He couldn't recall anything recently that could have led to Abhishek reacting like that.

Abhishek hesitated before meeting his eyes. “You’re so fucking mean, Ishan. I hate you.”

Ishan shot him a small smile, about to pull him in for a hug when Shubman burst into tears, almost scaring the other two.

“What the fuck areyoucrying for?” Abhishek hissed.

“Y-You can't h-hate Ish because of me. I’m the worst, hate me instead!” Shubman wailed, tears streaming down his cheeks as he sniffled and hiccupped.

“Okay, I’ll just hate you then,” Abhishek deadpanned.

“No! I don't want you to h-hate me, please,” he sobbed, clinging to Abhishek as if his life depended on it.

“Get off me,” Abhishek grunted, trying to push him away, but Shubman just shook his head furiously. “You're getting snot on my shirt, oh god. I’ll kill you!”

“I’m really sorry,” Shubman exclaimed. “I know I've been an idiot. You've always been there for me, and I couldn't have made it without you. I’m sorry if I ever made you feel like you were any less of a best friend to me than Ishan. I can't imagine my life without either of you. You’re my best friend, Abhi.My star.Please forgive me.”

Abhishek had never been good at holding grudges, especially when it came to someone he loved. So when Shubman cried on his shirt again, just like he had three years ago after Ishan left, Abhishek couldn't bring himself to push him away.

“You were born of love, Abhi,” his mother said, gently stroking his hair as he looked up from her lap. “Your father loved me, and I love you. I hope you’re always around people you love, and if not, I hope you grow to love them. You’ll get hurt and bruised but there's beauty in still smiling. We're briefly on earth, and we are all we have. So, please be forgiving when you can. Please be soft. Please be gentle.”

The world is cruel, therefore I won't be.

The world is cruel, therefore I won't be.

The world is cruel, therefore I won't be.

Abhishek wiped away Shubman’s tears before placing a kiss on his forehead. “It's not okay but it will be,” he said.

Shubman pulled him into a desperate hug, clinging to him tightly. “I love you,” he whispered shakily.

“I love you too,” Abhishek muttered with a smile.

Ishan smiled fondly at his friends before joining the embrace, wrapping his arms around the two of them. “You’re such idiots.”

“Says the one who ghosted us for three years,” Shubman grumbled into Abhishek's shirt, his voice muffled.

“First wipe off your snot, ugly.”

“Who’re you calling ugly?!”

“Do you want a mirror now?”

Abhishek chuckled, watching the two bicker. Shubman let out a dramatic scoff, “Well, you kissed this ugly face so joke's on you.”

He really did have the worst timing. Ishan watched as Abhishek visibly froze, his eyes flickering between the two. “I…..WHAT?”

Ishan winced, moving back. “We were playing truth or dare.”

Abhishek gasped. “Don't you dare lie to me. You’re making that face when you lie!”

Ishan shot Shubman a glare, trying to avoid Abhishek’s paling expression. Shubman caught on, swiftly sandwiching Abhishek’s face between his hands.

“I’ll kiss you too, don't worry!”

“What the hell, get away!” Abhishek yelped, scrambling off the bed, with Shubman giggling as he chased after him.

Ishan took a deep breath, trying to calm his pounding heart. He knew Abhishek wouldn't let it go easily. He'd have to come up with something soon. For now, he laughed as he watched Abhishek scream for help, with an evil dimpled boy hovering over him.

“Behold the smooch of your life!”

“Not on my watch!”Mayank declared as he emerged from a pile of blankets on the next bed.

Ishan blinked. “....He was here the whole time?”

Oh. Abhishek totally forgot about it.

Shubman burst into a fit of giggles as he collapsed next to Abhishek, his eyes fixed on Mayank, who had a SpongeBob shorts wrapped around his head like a turban.

“Rome shall not fall on my watch,” Mayank slurred, raising his imaginary sword.

Ishan reached out to guide Mayank back down. “We’ll save Rome at dawn. Goodnight, Your Majesty.”

He scrunched his nose at the sight of Mayank drooling, shaking his head with a sigh before slipping into bed next to him.

Shubman pouted. “You're not sleeping with us?”

“What are we, kids?” Ishan snorted. “Besides…he'smy best friend too.”

Abhishek smiled, signaling Shubman not to intervene. Shubman nodded with a soft chuckle, wrapping his arms around Abhishek giddily, who promptly removed them with a huff.

Staring at the ceiling in the dim moonlight, Ishan made a silent promise to himself to be a better friend. He couldn't project his insecurities into his friendships, everyone was fighting a battle of their own after all.

Suddenly, he gasped, rather loudly.

“Who’s dead?” Shubman shot up.

“I might have forgotten Aditi at the club,” Ishan said weakly.

Abhishek laughed, “Just his relationship, Shub.”

Why was Ishan so bad at being good? He cursed under his breath as he waited for Aditi. He decided that as soon as she came out, he would get down on his knees and beg her for forgiveness.

“Ishan!”

He looked up at her, noticing her eyebrows knitted in concern. “Are you okay? Is he okay?”

Ishan sighed in relief as she didn't seem to be mad at him. “Who?”

“Shubman.”

Ishan frowned, “How do you know about the fight? Even I didn't.”

“What are you talking about?”

“What areyoutalking about?”

Aditi held his hand, trying to comfort him. “Ish, there’s a photo circulating among the gossipmongers. Someone saw Shubman go into the restroom with a boy.”

Ishan’s heart dropped. “What's the big deal? Can't people go at the same time now?” he managed weakly.

“You know how jobless people are,” Aditi said with a sigh. “They just need drama. Plus, it doesn't help that they were holding hands.”

Ishan looked around, noticing a few eyes on them. Aditi gripped his hand tighter, sensing his discomfort. “I… I don't get it. What's wrong with holding hands?”

Aditi's eyes softened at his pleading voice. “Ignore them, Ish. They're judging without knowing anything.”

“W-Why are people looking at me like that, Adi?”

Aditi paused before saying, “Some asshole thought it would be fun to claim it was you.”

Ishan's eyes widened. “It wasnotme! Even if it was–”

“I know,” Aditi nodded. “It's just convenient gossip to drag the captains into it.”

Ishan’s head hurt. He could feel eyes boring into his back, hear the whispers around him. He hated hearing Shubman’s name from their filthy mouths. He had to stop the fire before it spread. He had to do something.

Something terrible.

Something like kissing Aditi in front of everyone.

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Title: CH: 21
Chapter_Text:
a/n:a lot of you didn’t get notified yesterday so just a heads-up to check out if you’ve read the last chp since this is its continuation.

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Aditi barely recovered from the kiss when Ishan blurted out, “Slap me.”

“W-What?”

Ishan darted his eyes around before repeating in a whisper, “Come on, just slap me.”

“Why–”

“Please, Aditi.”

She, still in a daze, nodded slowly upon hearing the desperation in his voice. With a mixture of confusion and hesitation, her hand slowly moved forward and then landed on Ishan's cheek.

Ishan could hear a few gasps around them, but his eyes lingered on the water bottle in Aditi's bag. “Now, grab that and splash it on me,” he said, pointing.

“What happened, Ish–”

“Please,” he mumbled, his eyes wide with urgency.

Aditi’s heart clenched as she shakily grabbed her water bottle. Her eyes locked with his as she twisted open the cap. Ishan shot her a weak smile, staying still as everyone pulled out their phones to record the scene.

Oh.

Aditi hesitated for a moment, her grip tightening around the bottle. She remembered something she had read:"In love, you are selfless."With that thought, she finally emptied the bottle over his head.

Louder gasps followed and the clicks intensified as people watched water drip down Ishan’s hair, soaking his clothes.

“Leave me,” Ishan whispered.

Aditi knew he meant for her to leave him just for the moment, but she felt tears brimming in her eyes. “Yeah,” she managed a small smile. “I will. Or you might never really find love.”

Ishan's heart ached as he watched her smile, but he resisted the urge to follow her. He allowed people to record him, giving them the gossip that should keep them talking for at least a week.

As long as Shubman’s secret was safe.

Ishan got used to the stares and whispers by the end of the day. He even flipped off a few people for good measure.

“Heard you got dumped, Kishan?”

Ishan sighed inwardly as he recognized the owner of the voice. He turned with a bright smile. “Heard your girlfriend fucked the football team, Rohan?”

Rohan clenched his jaw, his lips curving with anger. "You think you're funny."

Ishan just shrugged. “I would love to stay and chat, but you know, one of us has to win matches.”

Rohan’s fists clenched by his sides as he inched closer to Ishan. “You’re asking for it today,bitch,” he threatened, or at least tried to.

Ishan barely spared an eye-roll when he got shoved against the wall. He could end this before it began with an uppercut, but he would rather not get suspended few months before graduation. Instead, he smirked at Rohan.

“Still mad you couldn't even make it to the state?” he teased.

Rohan snarled, his face contorted with rage as he lunged forward, though he punched the wall instead in frustration. Ishan didn't bat an eye.

"Must be sad to be so pent-up. Maybe ask your girlfriend?” Ishan taunted mockingly. “Oops, too bad she's busy in the streets.”

“Fuck it, you're dead today,” Rohan hissed, his knuckles white as he grabbed Ishan's shirt and reeled him in. His fist collided with Ishan's jaw, the impact sending Ishan staggering backward. He could taste blood in his mouth for a second.

“Ah, fuck,” Ishan grunted as he spat. “Motherfucker, you think this is a movie?”

Rohan stood back, jutting his chin out. He pulled out his phone, showing the photo from last night. It wasn't even Ishan in the picture, but the implication was enough to make him feel sick to his stomach.

“Still got any balls or did you chop them off to be with that ***?”

That's when Ishan lost it. His eyes shot up in fury, and before he realized it, he had swung his fist, landing a solid punch squarely on Rohan's face.

Shubman was really worried about Ishan. First, he woke up to the news of his best friend apparently getting publicly dumped. Then, he couldn't find the boyanywhere. He wasn't even picking his calls or replying to his texts. The strangest thing was that he missed practice. Ishan never missed practice.

Shubman felt angry as he watched the video. It feltpersonal. How could Aditi do that to Ishan? He was going to talk to her, there was no other way. She could not get away with hurting his best friend like that.

When he finally saw Aditi, she looked...normal. That pissed Shubman off. She didn't deserve to sit around in peace while Ishan was probably off somewhere blaming himself.

“What are you playing at?” Shubman asked bluntly, not bothering to beat around the bush.

Aditi turned to him calmly. “Have you talked to him?”

“I would, if he wasn't fuckingmissing,” Shubman sneered. “What did he do to deserve that, Aditi? Didn't take you for a bitch.”

“It's not my place to say anything,” she muttered, averting her gaze.

“I need answers.” Shubman clenched his jaw, leaning in closer. “Because I have no idea where Ishan is and what he's doing to himself. Do you know how fucked up his head is?”

“Go to hell, Shubman. His head is not fucked up,” Aditi snapped. “Have you ever reallytalkedto him? Asked him what he's so afraid of? You think you know him so well, Shubman. But you don't.”

“Andyoudo?”

“God, Shubman, it's not about youorme! It's about Ishan.” Aditi ran her fingers through her hair exasperatedly. “I care about him–”

“You don't humiliate someone you care about like that,” Shubman scoffed in disbelief. “Tell me, what did he do? Is it because he left you at the club yesterday? Just because you had to go back alone for once? Can you notbreathewithout his attention?”

“You're one to say," she gritted before turning away.

“Yeah, walk away!” Shubman jeered. “Like a fucking coward. You don't deserve him anyway.”

Aditi stopped in her tracks, clenching her fists. “Andyoudo?” she asked, turning back to face him.

“What?” Shubman scowled. “Where do I fit into this equation?”

“You think I’m blind like both of you?”

“What the hell is that supposed to mean?”

Aditi stepped closer, her gaze steady as she looked up at him. “He asked me to do that.”

“What?”

“Whatever happened in the video, he asked me to do it. He wanted people to record him.”

Shubman stared at her, incredulous. “Do you take me for an idiot? Why would he want people to talk about him?”

“So that people don't talk aboutyou!So thatyourcarelessness doesn't getyouin trouble. So thatyoudon't have to hear anything remotely hurtful while he will happily be the jester. All foryou,” Aditi retorted, her voice trembling. “You do know that Ishan’s name was dragged into the messyoumade last night, right?”

Title: CH: 22
Chapter_Text:
Ishan was more confused than heartbroken as he watched Shubman leave. He wanted to call out, stop him, but his throat felt parched. Was this how Shubman had felt whenheleft? Why was everyone always leaving in this story? Where were they going?

After seconds of staring into the hallway, as if hoping Shubman would suddenly reappear, he finally remembered to breathe.

“Why does it feel like I just got… rejected?” he mumbled.

Aditi let out an amused huff. “Does that mean you were about to confess?”

Ishan looked back at her, realizing what he had said. His cheeks flushed in embarrassment. “W-What? No, I was just talking about the general… aura of the situation.”

Aditi sighed, making him drape his arm around her neck for support. “Okay, hero, let’s get you some first-aid.”

Ishan winced as he pushed himself onto his feet. “I’m sorry I involved you in this shit,” he said, a hint of sadness in his tone.

“Hm. You do have quite a lot to be sorry about, Ish,” she teased.

A silence settled between them, the cool evening breeze kissing Ishan's bruises as they walked down the hallway. He hesitated for a moment before speaking again. "I didn't lie to you. I really did like you," he admitted softly.

“I know.” Aditi smiled before looking away. “But you didn't love me.”

There was a pause before Ishan whispered, “I couldn't.”

It's as if his soul didn'tallowhim to. It had known all along, long before he did, what it meant to really love someone. There was no sudden epiphany, no dramatic realization. It wasn't a matter of love at first sight or anything of that sort. Instead, it was familiarity. Like:“Oh, it's you. I’m glad it's you.”

“It's okay,” Aditi chuckled. “People act like it's the end of the world when someone doesn't love you back.”

“Is it not?” Ishan asked weakly, glancing at her.

Aditi hummed. “Would you give up cricket if you lost an important match?”

“No,” Ishan replied, scrunching his nose. “I mean, I would be sad, but I don't play cricket with any expectations. I play because I love it.”

“I, too, didn't love you in hopes of you returning it,” Aditi said with a warm smile.

Ishan gulped, feeling like he would turn into water if he looked at her any longer. “I’m sorry,” was all he could say.

“I am not.”

“What?”

“I am not sorry for loving you. Don't make me feel like I should be. Don't look at me like I’m a tragedy.”

Ishan nodded, reaching to place a light kiss on the crown of her head. “I’ll always be there for you, Adi.”

“I know.” Aditi cracked a genuine smile, leading him into the infirmary.

Maybe that's what it was; sometimes you meet people and things don't work out, but that doesn't mean they have to leave. Or you have to leave. For Ishan, living under the same sky as Aditi was enough.

For Aditi, wishing to meet Ishan again in another life was enough.

“Your taste in men is questionable though,” Aditi said when she walked Ishan to his dorm.

Ishan spluttered, his cheeks warming with embarrassment. "W-What are you talking about?”

Aditi shot him an unimpressed look. “Really? After calling him your moon?Really, Ish?”

“I said that out loud?” Ishan squeaked. “He didn't hear it, right?”

Aditi rolled her eyes, sighing. “Good night, Ishan.”

“Adi,” he called out with a small smile, his eyes reflecting hope. “We are…”

Aditi met his gaze with a soft smile. “Friends,” she replied without any hesitation.

“KILL ME! KILL ME RIGHT NOW!”

Yashasvi stared at his roommate. “Do I get to be the Captain then?”

Shubman ignored him, continuing to kick the punching sack. “I’m gonna jump. That's right, gonna flip off the terrace.”

“The terrace is closed at night,” Yashasvi said, glancing at the time.

Shubman glared at him. “You're getting benched in the next game!”

Yashasvi snorted. “Your season’s over, Cap. We lost toSummit, remember?”

“Don't take that name,” Shubman hissed, flopping on his bed.

It was safe to say that he was having an existential crisis. He couldn't shake the image from his mind: Ishan, with a bloody nose and bruised face, smiling softly at him. It seemed like if he spoke any more, Shubman would have cried. Whatever his next words were going to be, Shubman couldn't have bore the weight of them.

So, he ran away. As if they were in a fucking chick-flick. As if they would never have to meet again. As if he could live without seeing Ishan.

He let out an exasperated groan, then glanced at Yashasvi, who was still staring at him. “What?” he asked pointedly.

“You’re gay.”

“At least make it sound like a question!”

Shubman sighed. It was only for so long his teammates could pretend to not have a whiff of the rumours. However, he didn't expect Yashasvi to be the first one to bring it up.

He glanced nervously at the boy. “Um, do you think it's… weird?”

He wouldn't suddenly kick Shubman out of their room, right? Well, he couldn't. Shubman was his senior after all.

Yashasvi munched on his granola bar nonchalantly. “Do you think I give a fuck about you?”

Shubman smiled. After two years of living with the guy, he knew it meant:“I won’t judge you.”

“Right,” he cleared his throat. “But I’m kind of in a fix right now.”

Yashasvi, though looking disinterested, asked, “What happened?”

Shubman wasn't sure where to start. Everything in his life was tied to Ishan for some reason. He sighed, “The bottom-line is that we haven't been talking, me and Ishan.”

“What did you do?”

“Bad Yashu!” Shubman threw a pillow at him, which he promptly dodged. “I didn't do anything. I just ran away.”

“Huh? From where?”

“Well, you know… responsibility?”

Yashasvi blinked. “So you fought.”

“Not exactly,” Shubman winced. “He fought with someone. For me.”

“I don't see the problem. Wouldn't you have done the same for him?”

“Yeah, but,” Shubman bit his lip. “He got hurt because of me. I…”

He let out a frustrated grunt, staring at his own hands. “Someone always gets hurt because of me. Be it Ishan, or Abhishek, or my dad, or my team.”

“Well, that escalated quickly,” Yashasvi muttered. He cleared his throat before looking at the other. “Look, I’m not really good at this, and I don't know what to say about your current situation. But I do know that you’ve never let our team down. You're the best captain we could've asked for. Um, thanks for taking me to the finals.”

Shubman stared at him, feeling an odd feeling of warmth wash over him. He was used to Yashasvi’s cold and curt replies, not whatever this was. So he let out a deliberate whistle. “Yashu, I didn't know you were such a softie!”

Yashasvi let out a sound closer to a growl (not beating the tsundere cat allegations) as his cheeks flushed. “Drop dead,” he said before throwing a pillow at the other.

Shubman chuckled as he flopped down on the bed again. Maybe he needed to spend more time with his own team. He should learn that from Ishan; it wasn't him or Abhishek who brought back the light in Ishan. You don't always get comfort from the people you want it from.

Who knows? Maybe Virat could be a good love guru. Not that Shubman needed any love advice, certainly not any concerning his best friend.

"I think you two are in love," Virat said, casually popping a grape into his mouth.

Shubman shot him a flat look. “I want solutions, not more complications.”

“That's exactly what’s complicating your situation, though.”

Shubman sighed, turning to Rahul. “What do you think I should've done?”

Rahul shrugged. “Not run away, maybe. What's the most he could've said?”

"I don't know! It was like looking at the sun directly. I just couldn't stand there and pretend like nothing had changed between us.”

“What changed?” Druv asked, narrowing his eyes.

Shubman darted his eyes around nervously. “Um, you know, us. Things happened between us–”

“Whatthings?” Dhruv pressed.

Shubman felt his face getting warmer at the memory of kissing Ishan. “Nothing much…”

“Did you fuck him?” Shreyas chimed in, causing Yashasvi to cover Dhruv’s ears.

“NO!” Shubman’s heart almost jumped out at the thought.

“Didhefuck you?” Kuldeep offered.

“God, no! What is wrong with you guys?” Shubman exclaimed, looking terrified.

Virat hummed, stroking his stubble. “But you didsomething, didn't you?”

Shubman rubbed the back of his neck, feeling embarrassment crawling up his bones. “We might have k-kissed.”

Everyone gasped. Shubman groaned, “Oh, come on! As if you guys weren't just suggesting worse.”

Virat grinned mischievously. “Ishan and Shubman, sitting in a tree, K-I-S-S-I-N-G. First come love, then comes marriage–”

“Shut up,” Shubman whined. “You guys are useless.”

Shami, who had been quiet, finally spoke. “So you like him?”

Shubman’s eyes widened. “I- don’t- can’t- I mean. God, I don't know!”

“Why’d you kiss him then, idiot?” Yashasvi said, rolling his eyes.

“Hekissed me first, the first time.”

“First time?” Shreyash quirked an eyebrow. “There was a second?”

“And a third,” Shubman muttered under his breath.

Virat giggled. “Ishan and Shubman sitting in a tree–”

“SHUT UP!”

Shreyash looked at Shubman, shooting him a sympathetic glance. “Chances are, either you like him, or you saw him as a sexual gratification.”

Shubman frowned. “I would never– I think we were both just trying to get a rise out of each other. Since we wereenemiesat the time, that could have been one way to rile each other up…?”

“Even I wouldn't believe that,” Shami mumbled.

“It happens in movies, doesn't it?” Shubman asked, pleading for anyone to agree with him.

Yashasvi scoffed. “Yeah and they end up together,moron.”

Rahul rested a comforting hand on Shubman's shoulder. “Look, people respond to their feelings in different ways. You can accept them and live your truth, or you can deny them and call it a day.Oryou can accept them and still not act on them. What is it that bothers you about the idea of liking him?”

Shubman sighed, tugging at his hair. “I mean, it'sIshan. We've grown together, seen each other at our best and worst. Our friendship has always been so… intense. I’m afraid of knowing how much more it can get if we, you know. It isoverwhelming.”

“I smell commitment issues,” Yashasvi whispered before Dhruv smacked him.

“No,” Shubman grunted. “I just… I don't know. It's like I didn't expect him to actually like me–”

“Hold up,” Dhruv said. “Did he say he likes you?”

“Well, he… um.”

Everyone stared at him expectantly.

“.... did not.”

Virat burst out laughing first, the others following suit. “That's embarrassing,” Yashasvi said.

Shubman blushed furiously. “I can't explain it to you guys! It's the way he waslookingat me. Sometimes you just know, alright?”

“What are you, a middle schooler?” Kuldeep snickered.

Shubman bit the inside of his cheek. Was he really being delusional? He wasn't a complete idiot to not realize how they had crossed the boundary of friendship more than once. He knew Ishan wouldn't do that to just any friend; he wasn't that kind of person.  Why did they have to be so close? The lines were so blurry, they might as well not exist.

Rahul smiled at him, as if trying hard not to pity him. “Do youwanthim to like you?”

Shubman’s mouth gaped a little. “I- I don't know. Probably not.”

“Wouldn’t you mind if he dated someone else then?” Virat asked, crossing his arms.

“He’s already dating Aditi.”

“Didn't he get dumped?”

Shubman winced at the memory, a knot of guilt tightening in his chest. “That was just an act–”

"They broke up," Yashasvi declared suddenly, showing his phone. "Rishabh bhai just texted me.”

Shubman gulped. Virat nudged him with a knowing smirk, “What a coincidence, isn't it?”

His heart started racing for whatever reason. “Whatever. I’m not seeing him until graduation anyway,” he muttered.

Yashasvi grinned as his eyes remained on his screen. “Oh, sweet! Apparently they have a practice game with us next month.”

That was two months before graduation. Virat slung an arm around Shubman, smirking. “Looks like cupid’s got different plans, buddy.”

Shubman wanted to kill himself.

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Title: CH: 23
Chapter_Text:
As the summer heat gently seeped through the cracks of the old sheds and cicadas rose to their full glory, Ishan learned to get a hold of his feelings which trickled down his chest, settling warmly in the pit of his stomach, like the clouds in the clear blue skies.

Summer dissolved in his mouth, and he, in love.

Staying away from Shubman wasn't as hard as Ishan had imagined. He was still getting used to the idea of loving his best friend in a way he had missed out on for so many years. So, if he caught himself daydreaming about Shubman once in a while, well, no one had to know.

With graduation edging closer, he was busy recruiting new players for the cricket club and trying to decide on a new captain to recommend to the coach. He kept himself busy, but at night, he couldn't resist the pull of the moon that would draw his thoughts to a certain dimpled boy.

He missed Shubman, that was undeniable. But he also felt a twinge of bashfulness at the thought of facing him. What would he even say? It wasn't like anything had happened, yet at the same time, it felt like everything had. He couldn't expect Shubman to understand; after all, he was the dumbest one in their trio. But talking to Abhishek felt equally embarrassing.

So, he turned to Mayank instead,gentlypeeling off the bandaid. “Do you think it's possible to fall in love with your best friend after years of just being friends?”

Mayank licked his popsicle thoughtfully before sighing. “I’m flattered, Ish. But I like someone else.”

Ishan shot him a flat look before the words sank in. “Wait, you like someone?” he exclaimed, catching Mayank in a headlock. “How dare you not tell me this before?”

Mayank grunted, trying to push Ishan away. “What a hypocrite! Why didn't you tell me about Shubman–”

“Who said anything about Shubman?” Ishan let him go with a huff. “Pehle tu bata,who's the unlucky girl?”

"Who's unlucky? What are you two talking about?" Abhishek asked, flopping on the grass next to them.

Mayank quietly returned to eating his popsicle as Ishan scoffed. “The girl he likes,” he said, pointing at the other.

Abhishek looked at Mayank (who promptly avoided his gaze) before absentmindedly plucking a few strands of dandelions from the grass, twirling them between his fingers. “Oh,” he muttered. “Unlucky indeed.”

Ishan frowned at the silence that followed. “Anyways, I have a problem,” he spoke first. “Shubman dipped on me. Kind of.”

Abhishek groaned, “What is it with you two? Give me a break!”

“It's not my fault,” Ishan defended himself. “You know how he ran away that day, and he hasn't called or texted since.”

Abhishek sighed. “Itisodd. He can't live a day without your attention.”

Ishan’s cheeks flushed a little. “Apparently, he can. It's been three weeks now.”

“Good for you that we’re going to see them next weekend,” Mayank said.

“What's good about that?” Ishan scoffed as he got up, avoiding any further questions.

Ishan was about ready to knock some sense into that pole. He had his reasons for not reaching out, but why was Shubman avoiding him? Ishan thought it was just another one of Shubman's distant phases, something he got into sometimes. They were random and shouldn't be taken personally. But now, Shubman was blatantly ignoring his existence. Scowling to himself, Ishan pulled out his phone and stared at the unanswered texts he had sent over the past few weeks.

youare you ok?

you really left?did something happen?it’s been a weekwhat is up you’re scaring meseen toh kar madarchoddid i do something?gaand mara bhai

He loved Shubman, but God, was he fucking annoying? Ishan had gotten used to the radio silence after two weeks, deciding he would rather confront him head-on.

Abhishek cleared his throat, glancing at Mayank. “Who is it?” he asked.

Mayank blinked. “Huh?”

“The girl you like.”

Mayank’s face felt hot as he fumbled for the right words. “Why do you care?”

“I don't,” Abhishek snorted.

“Fine.”

“Fine.”

After a long stretch of awkward silence, Mayank got up as well. “Come on, I’ll throw you some balls in the nets,” he said, looking down at the other.

Abhishek stared at him, his expression unreadable. “Pussy,” he muttered as Mayank walked away.

“I heard that!”

“That was the intention,” Abhishek yelled after him despite the small smile playing at the corners of his lips.

“Horses! Tillu, they have horses!” Rishabh exclaimed as they strolled through the campus.

“Why do you always act like this when we're here?” Ishan chuckled, turning to Abhishek, who looked a little slumped. He nudged the other playfully. “You okay?”

Abhishek nodded, his gaze fixed longingly on the cricket field. He could still see himself padded up, shouting at Shubman to hurry up, chasing after Dhruv for pulling yet another prank, trying to stop Yashasvi from killing someone, admiring Virat in the nets… Well, he could go on forever. He hadn't had the chance to properly miss the place since Ishan came back into his life, but now that he was here, he felt like a stray dog. Maybe he always was. BecauseMeridianhadn't been kind to him, or to anyone for that matter. Yet, it had changed him for the worse, and Shubman, for the better.

He found a home in his new friends. But he couldn't leave his burning house after he had spent hours cleaning its floors.

“Do you regret coming toSummit?” Ishan's voice pulled him out of his thoughts.

Abhishek shook his head. “How can I ever regret meeting you?”

Ishan grinned, ruffling the other's hair. “Now let's go and beat their asses again.”

"Who are we beating?" Hardik popped his head in. "I didn't bring my hockey stick.”

Ishan swatted at his arm. “Maybe use thatenthusiasmin the match, alright? Did you see Shubman?”

“I think he was arguing with someone,” Hardik grunted. “Markande, probably.”

Ishan frowned, hoping it wasn't about what he had said to Mayank earlier. He would have rushed down to intervene, but Rohit called him to talk about the match.

Meanwhile, Shubman felt like a child caught in the act. Normally, he wouldn't take any shit from Mayank and would just flip him off. But now, he barely had anything to defend himself by, so he resisted the urge to provoke the other. Besides, hurting Mayank would probably be the last straw for Ishan before he gave up on Shubman for good.

“You don't just ghost someone who got humiliated and into a fight for you,” Mayank said.

“I didn'tghosthim,” Shubman hissed. “I had to leave for reasons I don't owe you an explanation for.”

“Give meone, one good reason why you didn't bother to respond to his calls or texts for three fucking weeks.”

“I’m-!” Shubman pinched the bridge of his nose, huffing. “I’m not telling you, Mayank. Who are you to ask me anyway?”

“His best friend,” Mayank scoffed. “Who acts like one.”

Shubman ran his hand through his hair, sighing. “Jesus, you're annoying as ever. Fine. I’ll talk to him, alright?”

“Don't make it sound like you're doing him a favour,” Mayank warned. “Grow some balls, or eyes. Clearly, you don't have either.”

Shubman clenched his fists, feeling his cheeks heat up. He forced every muscle in his body to remain still, for the sake of his own sanity. “Sorry,” he mumbled.

“Tell that to him.”

That's how Shubman ended up approaching Ishan before the match. Ishan put on a good show of not noticing him until Shubman was up close in his face.

“Hey, Shubman,” Ishan said in feign cheer. “Didn't see you there.”

Shubman winced slightly, rubbing the back of his neck. “Mad enough to use my full name, huh?”

Ishan's smile only widened. “I’m going to kill you in the match today.”

“Um,” Shubman gulped. It could be fatal when Ishan channeled his anger into his cricket. “I’m really sorry, Ish. I’ll make it up to you.”

That wiped the smile off Ishan’s face. “I just need the reason, Shubman,” he said.

Mayank and Ishan were eerily alike, Shubman thought. “I don’t know what to say, honestly,” Shubman muttered.

Ishan stared at him for a moment before starting to put on his pads. "Fine," he muttered.

Shubman crouched down beside Ishan, looking up at him meekly. “It's not, is it?”

Ishan met Shubman’s eyes with his own fierce ones. “Of fucking course it's not fine. I can ignore the part where you ran away for whatever reason, but I think I deserved at least a call, or a text," Ishan retorted.

Shubman's shoulders slumped as he looked down. From a distance, he probably looked like a puppy in the rain. “Ish–”

"Best friends don't treat each other like that. I'm not your girlfriend or boyfriend or," Ishan fumbled with frustrated hand gestures. “Whatever the hell you’ve got going on.”

“Will you be?”

“What?”

“....Whatever the hell I’ve got going on?” Shubman tried.

Ishan took a deep breath before turning over his shoulder to yell out to Shastri in the next hall. “Coach, can I kill someone?”

“Not fromSummit. We don't have substitutes today.”

“Got it.”

Shubman squealed as Ishan caught him with a headlock. “Mazak sujh raha hai tujhe behen ke laude.Did you have a good laugh while I was worrying about you?”

Shubman coughed at the chokehold, trying to wriggle out. “I can't breathe! This isnotsexy!”

A giggle escaped Ishan. He somehow managed to climb onto Shubman's back and forced him to give him a piggyback ride until the match started. Mayank shot Ishan a soft smile, and Shubman a glare.

The perks and cons of falling for your best friend included not being able to stay mad at them.

It felt like charity whenMeridianwon, but a win was a win– practice or real. Shubman wasn't overjoyed; especially because now, hehad toface Ishan.

"Yashu, don't you think you should spend some time with yourfavouritesenior?" Shubman asked, trying to keep him from leaving the room as Ishan smiled menacingly from the corner.

Yashasvi blinked. “Yes, that's why I’m going to Rohit bhaiya.”

“I hope you die!” Shubman yelled as the other simply walked out.

“I don't know about me, but you sure will,” came the reply before the door clicked close.

Shubman turned around, saying his prayers mentally. “Ish, do you want to–”

“No.”

“Mogu Mogu–”

“Come here, Shubman.”

Shubman obeyed, not wanting to risk testing Ishan’s temper. He shot his best puppy eyes at the other. “I’m sorry, Ish.”

Ishan was no amateur. He held his composure as he leaned in closer, twisting Shubman’s ear slightly. “What are you sorry for?” he whispered as his eyes stared into the other's closely.

Shubman felt his throat go dry. “F-For leaving without any explanation,” he mumbled.

Too close, too close, too close.

“And?” Ishan probed.

“A-And making you go through shit. You got hurt because of me and I couldn't deal with that,” he said the last part with a sad frown.

Ishan pulled back with a sigh, releasing his ear as well. “I would readily do it again for you, Shubi. Just…stay, alright? Don't leave me.”

Shubman nodded weakly. “I’m a fucking coward, that's just what it is.”

“What are you afraid of?” Ishan asked softly, reaching for Shubman’s hand.

“You– Ugh. You knowwhat.”

“I don't,” Ishan said genuinely. “What is it, meri jaan?”

Shubman’s pupils visibly dilated, his face softening. “Don't do this to me. Don't give me hope, Ishan.”

Ishan grinned,adorably, like he always did. “What are you talking about, Gill?”

“Ishan.”

“Hm?”

“I’m a mess.”

“Absolutely.”

“I'm scared of needing you.”

“Uh huh.”

“I’m an obtuse fucking asshole.”

“Wrong.” Ishan couldn't be smiling wider. “You'remyobtuse fucking asshole.”

Shubman groaned, burying his face in his hands. “Why did you break up with Aditi?” he asked after a minute of comfortable silence, peeking out slightly.

“I couldn't love her,” Ishan replied.

“Loveher?” Shubman muttered. “Or, loveher?”

Ishan chuckled. “What’re you trying to do, jaan?”

Shubman’s cheeks flushed. “Do you…”

“Hm?”

“Nevermind.”

Ishan smiled, glancing at the moon outside. “Do you think Icarus should have flown to the sun?” he asked.

Shubman frowned at the sudden question but replied, “Yes.”

“Why? He fell, didn't he?”

Ishan looked at him with hopeful eyes, and Shubman was back in their school library, where they sat giggling over a random book they picked, and at the right corner over the lines of a century-old poem where Ishan's fingers grazed with his own, his fingers felt like they were on fire, much like Icarus.

He had learned the poem that day without realizing, maybe for this exact moment when Ishan looked at him with his eyes that shone like pearls. Shubman thought, if he were Icarus, he would have been glad to fall into the sea, and he would have spent the rest of his life trying to find a pearl as beautiful as Ishan’s eyes.

“For all he had fallen, still he flew,” Shubman could recite from memory. “And for a moment, the sun knew of him, too.”

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

Title: CH: 24
Chapter_Text:
Ishan's eyes shone with such intensity that Shubman could have mistaken it for tears, if not for the fondest smile on his face.

Shubman cleared his throat. “Stop looking at me like that,” he muttered.

“Like what?”

“Like you are… um, constipated.”

Ishan blinked. “What?”

Shubman cursed himself mentally before adding, “Feelings. You seem constipated with feelings.”

Ishan wasn't sure whether to laugh at Shubman or knock him out. Leaning in, he grabbed a fistful of Shubman's shirt and pulled him closer. “Are we just going to ignore the elephant in the room then?”

Shubman’s breath hitched at the proximity, and he tried to avert his eyes. “I don't know what you're talking about,” he mumbled.

Ishan gave him a little jerk, forcing Shubman to look back at him. His gaze dropped to the other's lips before tracing back up to his eyes. “Should I show you?” he said, barely above a whisper.

Shubman felt parts of himself melting away. He took a sharp breath before managing to whisper, “Yes, please.”

As much as Ishan wanted to dive in roughly, to make their teeth and tongues clash, to have it wet and dirty– because that's how they did it all these times– he didn't. Instead, he slowly placed his lips over Shubman’s, and they met in a gentle, tender kiss. It was like a whisper, soft and sweet, filled with unspoken confessions. His hand reached up to cradle Shubman's jaw, while his other hand made comforting circles on Shubman's nape.

"I love you, I love you, I love you,"Ishan's mind repeated like a broken record.

"I love you," Shubman echoed as he pulled away, his breath coming out in a gasp.

Ishan smiled, his eyes tracing Shubman's face, his touches lingering over its soft corners. “Yeah, I know,” he replied softly.

Shubman's heart felt like it was about to leap out of his chest. He had never felt like this, not even when he lifted the first trophy forMeridian. He wasn't sure what expression to make.

“You knew?”

“Didn't you?”

Shubman stared at Ishan, who looked like the sun. He was fiery. Shubman was burning, hot wax trailed down his ankles and scorched his skin. He had never felt more alive. He was burning in Ishan's gaze, and he couldn't look away. He wouldn't look away.

"Yeah," Shubman said, releasing a breath he didn't realize he was holding. A bubbling laugh escaped him as he threw his head back. "Yeah, I did.”

How could he have not known when whole of his life had been about Ishan than himself? He had always known. Hissoulhad always known.

Ishan grinned, mirth shining in his eyes. “I love you too,” he said with a giggle.

Shubman had to bury his face in a pillow to hide the giddiness he felt. Ishan was just too cute for his heart to handle. He peeked out from behind his hands when he felt the other's weight on his body.

"Bad Shubi! You're supposed to hug me and never let me go," Ishan scolded playfully.

Shubman groaned to himself. Lord, have mercy on him. “Don't blame me if you die of suffocation,” he muttered before pulling Ishan down with him, rolling onto the bed. He blew raspberries on the crook of Ishan’s neck, causing him to burst into more giggles.

Shubman was disgusted and impressed alike at how his heart immediately turned into a mushy, gooey poodle for Ishan. “I’m going to eat you up,” he said, pressing kisses along the other's neck.

“In a sexual way or in a cannibal way?” Ishan wiggled his eyebrows. Shubman stared at him flatly before biting into Ishan’s arm, causing the other to squeal.

"You act like a puppy!" Ishan huffed, swatting him away.

Shubman giggled, nuzzling his head against Ishan's stomach. “Puppy demands attention and kisses,” he muttered.

Ishan scrunched his nose, trying to hold back a smile. “You're so cringe,” he said, his eyes overflowing with fondness.

“It’s too late to get rid of me now.”

“I don't want to,” Ishan said before he realized, and judging by the menacing grin on Shubman's face, he knew he was going to be teased about it all week.

“Did you find your phone?” Dhruv asked, eyeing Yashasvi worriedly. “Why do you look like you saw a ghost?”

“I should really learn to knock before entering,” Yashasvi muttered with a sigh.

“Did you walk in on someone wanking again?”

“No,” Yashasvi winced. “Gill finally grew the balls to… you know.”

Dhruv’s eyes shot wide. “He confessed?”

Yashasvi snorted. “Oh, he didwaymore than that.”

Dhruv was grinning from ear to ear like a schoolgirl waiting for gossip. “What were they doing?”

“Practically eating each other's faces.” Yashasvi’s cheeks flushed a little as he rolled his eyes. “They didn't even notice when the door clicked open.”

“Damn,” Dhruv whistled. “I’m proud of Shub bhaiya.”

“What's there to be proud of?” Yashasvi scoffed, even though he was a little proud too. “He’s going to be more annoying now. I’ll kick him out of the room if I wake up to babu-shona talks in the middle of nights.”

Dhruv chuckled, humming. “He doesn't seem like the type to keep his mouth shut, unlike Virat bhai when he started dating.”

Yashasvi grunted. Just two more months until he could have the room all to himself. “People get weird in relationships.”

Dhruv glanced at Yashasvi before idly playing with the hem of his shirt. “What kind of person are you in a relationship?”

“Huh?” Yashasvi blinked. “I don't know, I’ve never been in one.”

“Oh,” Dhruv beamed. “Do you want to?”

Yashasvi’s cheeks flared up, and his ears grew warm. “No,” he said with a scowl before walking away. “I don't have time for that when I should be practicing.Youshould too. So, you shouldn't get into a relationship either.”

Dhruv chuckled as he followed the other. “You're cute when you're embarrassed.”

“Shut up!”

The next morning, Ishan was a little confused by the knowing smirks Rishabh (Dhruv’s courtesy) kept shooting at him.

"Is there something on my face?" Ishan asked during lunch.

"Beauty," Shubman whispered next to him with a dreamy look on his face. Ishan shot him a disgusted look, even though their hands were joined under the table.

“Does anyone have a knife?” Yashasvi asked. When Shubman looked at him with a terrified expression, he added, “Butter knife.”

“I feel like he might really kill me one day,” Shubman muttered, turning back to Ishan.

“I’ll save you,” Ishan whispered with a cheesy smile.

“You’re too small,” Shubman said with a feign sigh.

“Don't say it till you see it,” Ishan whispered with a wink.

“Oh, really.” Shubman said. “Then, can I? Tonight?”

Yashasvi slammed his hands on the table. "This butter knife is too blunt!” he exclaimed.

Dhruv chuckled as Tilak pointed out, “That's why it’s called a butter knife,” looking confused.

Ishan shot one last smile at Shubman before turning to the rest of the group. “Do you guys want to go out tonight?”

“Yes!” Yashasvi responded first, almost desperately. “Yes,please.”

“Wow, Yashu wants to miss his extra practice?” Shreyash spoke. “Well, I have no excuse to deny then.”

Shubman couldn't believe Ishan would go as far as to send everyone away just to be with him. Was he finally gettingsometonight?

Apparently not.

"Why are you all dressed up?" Shubman asked, eyeing Ishan's black Amiri shirt which he of course stole from Shubman’s closet. He couldn't possibly be putting this much effort to get… well, fucked.

“Huh?” Ishan was busy styling his hair before he turned to look at Shubman. “Why are younotdressed up?”

“I thought it was supposed to be me and you tonight, just us.”

Ishan blinked, his cheeks flushing when he realized. “I wasn't planning anything like that,” he muttered. “I just thought it would be nice for everyone to hang out together.”

Shubman shot him an unimpressed look before locking the door behind him. “I definitely planned something,” he said.

Ishan’s heart skipped a beat before he returned to brushing his hair. “Yeah?” he asked inattentively. “What's that?”

Shubman walked up behind him, wrapping his arms around Ishan's waist. “You're not going anywhere,” Shubman whispered in his ear, inhaling the scent of Ishan’s cologne.

“But they're waiting–”

"I’ve waited longer," Shubman interjected. "Please touch me, or I might really go insane.”

Ishan bit his lip, fighting against his own excitement. He turned around to face Shubman, whose eyes were already in a trance. “Have you…. with anyone else?” he asked softly.

“No,” Shubman replied without any hesitation. He hadn't been intimate with anyone before, not beyond kissing and messy makeouts. He couldn't do it with a girl even though he had been on dates with some; that would be lying to the girls, and to himself. He couldn't do it with a boy either, that was too risky.

Ishan probably had some experience with the girlfriends he apparently had over the years. While that made Shubman a little jealous, he was more worried about disappointing Ishan in bed.

"Um, I haven't been with a man before. So, technically, I’m a virgin too?” Ishan blubbered. “Are you sure about this, Shubi?”

Shubman chuckled, planting a chaste kiss on the other's nose. “I’m always sure about you.”

Ishan smiled, his eyes crinkling with warmth as Shubman kissed him. They moved back until they hit the edge of the bed, slowly leaning back until Shubman was over him.

Ishan closed his eyes, losing himself in the kisses Shubman pressed along his neck, trailing down to his chest. His hands found their way to Shubman’s hair, running through it slowly.

“You're beautiful,” Shubman mumbled against his skin. “Ethereal.”

A loud moan filled Shubman's ears, causing him to look up at Ishan with amused eyes. “I haven't even done anything yet for you to moan like that.”

Ishan blinked. “That wasn't me.”

“What?”

Again. It seemed to be coming from the next room where Abhishek was staying. Well, it used to be his room until he leftMeridiananyway. Shubman and Ishan’s eyes widened at the second moan.

Both of them scrambled out of bed and dashed to the next room. God, why was the door left open? What kind of kink was that?

Abhishek and Mayank looked up at the intruders who gawked at the sight before them.

“Oh, you broke your arm,” Shubman muttered, watching Mayank return to fix Abhishek’s bandages. The latter let out another moan of pain, causing Shubman to mentally slap himself.

“You broke your arm?!” Ishan exclaimed, rushing to Abhishek’s side immediately. “How the hell did that happen?”

Mayank sighed. “It's not broken. He just fell down a couple stairs.”

“Once again, how the hell did that happen?”

Abhishek scowled. “I was just a little surprised because Mayank…”

Ishan turned to the guy, narrowing his eyes. “What did you do?”

"I just told him I liked him," Mayank said with a shrug, leaving everyone else in the room gaping.

Abhishek blushed in embarrassment. “How can you say that out loud?!”

“What? They're our friends,” Mayank grumbled. “Besides, didn't you want me to stop being a pussy?”

“Hold up, I need some time to process this,” Shubman muttered. He couldn't even be mad anymore about being cockblocked.

Ishan couldn't help the laugh that escaped him. Everything was so ridiculous. “So, the one we were talking about that day was Abhi?”

Mayank shot him a sheepish smile before nodding. Ishan grinned at Abhishek, “Well, isn't that convenient? Don't you dare reject my best friend.”

“You– Ugh!” Abhishek’s cheeks flushed a darker shade of red. “Get out, all of you.”

“He's a little shy,” Mayank whispered to Ishan.

“That's so cute!” Ishan whispered back.

Shubman snorted. He slapped Mayank’s back playfully before saying, “Oi, monkey. If you dare hurt my Abhi, count your fucking days.”

Mayank shot him a sarcastic smile. “You too, if you hurt my Ish.”

“He's not yours–”

“Abhi is not yours too–”

Ishan frowned at something he realized. “Wait. Mayu, how do you know about us? It’s not even been a day.”

Shubman muttered an “oh”. He should really be more observant, like his boyfriend (boyfriend, hehe).

“Do you see yourselves?” Abhishek spoke with a scoff. “You look like you were in the middle of fucking.”

Only then did Ishan's gaze fall on Shubman's hair, now tousled from their earlieractivity. His lips looked plump from all the kissing and biting. Ishan didn't even want to imagine his own condition but Mayank's disgusted look gave it away. Ishan sighed exasperatedly as silence filled the room.

“We should draw dicks on Abhi's bandages,” was the ice breaker.
