Title: CH: 25
Chapter_Text:
“Shubman, I’m getting late,” Ishan said as though he didn't want to miss the bus and spend the day with his boyfriend. “Let me go, you sap.”

“No.” Shubman’s voice came out muffled as he kept kissing the base of Ishan’s neck. “I won't see you for another two months. Let me have my moment.”

Ishan smiled, reaching out to run his hands through Shubman’s hair. “What are you doing after finals?”

“I'm coming to you first thing after writing my last paper.”

Ishan rolled his eyes. “Youknowwhat I mean, Shubi.”

Shubman let out a small groan, dropping his head on Ishan’s shoulder. “I don't know. Can we not think about the future?”

“Have you heard from the scouts?” Ishan prodded.

Shubman shook his head, his hair tickling Ishan’s neck. “Have you?”

“Not yet,” Ishan muttered. “I don't want to admit it but I think I’m… scared.”

Shubman sat upright and looked at the other. He reached out, cupping Ishan's face. “You will make it.”

It wasn’t assurance, or comfort for that matter. Shubman’s eyes shone like he had never been surer of anything else. It wasbelief.

“Yeah,” Ishan smiled. “We will.”

Shubman grinned, showing off his dimples, then leaned in to peck Ishan’s lips. Again, and again, and again. Ishan giggled after each kiss, making Shubman melt into a puddle.

“I’ll miss you,” Shubman whispered, his forehead pressed against Ishan’s.

“I won't.”

“You think you're funny,” Shubman grumbled.

“Think? Iknow,” Ishan replied with a wide grin, dodging Shubman’s kiss so it landed on his cheek instead.

Shubman let out a small whine, trying for another kiss. Ishan laughed, turning his head just in time to catch Shubman's lips with his own.

He leaned into Shubman's touch as the other's hand slipped under his shirt. Ishan shivered slightly as cool fingertips traced patterns on his skin, sending tingles down his spine.

Shubman's warm breath tickled Ishan's ear as he whispered, "Like it?" after trailing his lips up Ishan's neck.

Ishan nodded weakly as Shubman's hands explored the curves and dips of his body. He wished they could get rid of the clothes but he didn't want to come off as desperate.

Shubman abruptly withdrew his hands and got up, prompting Ishan to shoot him a glare, annoyed at the sudden loss of touch. “Wipe that stupid grin off your face.”

Shubman pulled his boyfriend up, planting a big smooch on Ishan’s cheek. The latter couldn't help but crack a fond smile.

“Come on, we're going on a date,” Shubman said, grinning from ear to ear.

Ishan's cheeks flushed. The realization that they were now boyfriends (boyfriends!!!) hadn't fully sunk in yet. He wasn't sure how to act on a date with Shubman of all people. Nothing could have prepared him for this moment.

“Date,” he repeated dumbly. “Really?”

Shubman chuckled, mentally cooing at the red cheeks. “Yes, baby.”

If Ishan was blushing before, he was practically on fire now. If this were a cartoon, steam would be billowing from his ears like a kettle about to burst.

“Disgusting,” he muttered. “Say it again.”

Shubman's cheeks were starting to ache from all the smiling, but he couldn't help but grin even wider. "Would you do me the honor of going on a date with me, my baby?”

Ishan was too embarrassed to reply, so he just said, “I’m borrowing your shirt.”

Shubman knew he wouldn't get it back, just like he wouldn't get his heart back from the boy wearing it.

"Your idea of a date is to race three miles?" Ishan squinted up at the winding hill road behind Meridian.

Shubman nodded, seemingly proud of his idea. “Isn't it awesome?”

Ishan turned to him with bored eyes before breaking into a sly grin. “Fuck yeah! Don't be a sore loser,” he said, slapping Shubman's back.

"Hah? Who said I’m going to lose?” Shubman stretched his legs, warming up.

Ishan smirked, looking ahead– “You're not going to lose, Shubman.Iam going to win.” –With that, he lunged forward, and Shubman swiftly followed suit, shouting about Ishan always cheating.

"Why are you laughing?" Shubman yelled as they breezed past each other every second, the wind whipping through their hair.

"I love summer,” Ishan yelled back, his laughter echoing like a song in Shubman's ears.

“What?”

"I love summer,” Ishan repeated. “And you.”

Shubman loved and hated summers. They had a logic all their own, weighing down his heart and clinging to him like the heady stains of sin-black cherries. And he felt unlovable, like sun-bleached wild grass, and found god in the untamed weeds.

For a long time, he hadn’t allowed himself to be loved. Not in the way he had dreamed of while sitting in the corner of his tenth-grade classroom, eyes tracing the rough hands and crooked smiles of other boys. He wished summer would end soon so the boys would be back in their winter uniforms. Maybe then he wouldn't feel sodirty.

Shubman stared at Ishan, the unashamed crinkles by his eyes that formed crescents as his laughter rang out unbridled. As they reached the other side, both out of breath and grinning like fools, Shubman knew that for once, he didn't want summer to end.

“Spend all your summers with me,” Shubman blurted.

“Sounds like you're proposing,” Ishan teased.

“That's not– I–”

“Don't wory, the answer is yes.”

Shubman rubbed his palms against the fabric of his pants. “To?”

“Both.”

"Noted," Shubman murmured, his lips trembling as he tried to suppress a wide grin.

Ishan's breath was still heavy from the run, brushing sweat from his brow. "Who won?" he asked, his eyes twinkling.

Shubman knew he had lost. "I did," he said anyway.

“No way,” Ishan scoffed, shaking his head. “You’re supposed to cross this,” he added, rubbing his shoe over the imaginary finish line drawn on the concrete.

“I still won,” Shubman said, staring fondly at his boyfriend.

Ishan scrunched his nose when he caught onto the implication. “So corny,” he muttered, though the blush creeping up his cheeks betrayed his words.

Shubman claimed it was still a part of their date when he took Ishan to a lakeside park— one where Ishan would have probably sat judging couples with Rishabh. It was funny to think that someone else might be judging him and Shubman now, especially as the latter fed him a kulfi.

"I have a feeling that Rishabh knows about us," Ishan remarked, lapping up the stray trail of kulfi that had melted onto his finger.

Shubman hummed. “Do you not want anyone to know?”

“I don't really mind if our friends find out,” Ishan murmured. “But I do feel a bit guilty about not telling Aditi.”

“God, I feel bad for her.”

Ishan raised an eyebrow. “Really?”

Shubman rubbed his neck sheepishly. “No.”

Ishan let out a sigh. He couldn't shake off the guilt of potentially hurting Aditi, especially considering how much she had supported him through two major crises in his life.

“I don't want to hide anything from her,” Ishan said after a pause. “But sometimes I feel like I'm still dealing with the fact that I’m into guys.”

“Guys?” Shubman asked, narrowing his eyes. “Plural?”

Ishan snorted. “I’m trying to be serious here, Shubi.”

Shubman chuckled, casually looping an arm around Ishan's shoulder. "Sorry, let's just let loose for sometime, okay? You can go back to being a chronic overthinker without me when I'm not around for two whole months.”

Ishan nodded with a small smile as they fell into a comfortable silence, listening to the sounds around them; the gentle lapping of the lake’s water, the distant hum of traffic, and the murmurs of other lovers. As the sun dipped toward the horizon, Ishan found its glow just as hopeful as the dawn's first light.

"Let's join the national team together," Shubman said, and Ishan felt a rush of nostalgia as he realized how long it had been since he heard those words.

The chances were slimmer than ever, Ishan knew that. But seeing the way Shubman's eyes shone, much like the reflection of the setting sun glimmering on the lake, all Ishan could manage was, "Yeah. Let's.”

When Ishan returned toSummit, he swung by the coach’s office to get a copy of the new recruits list.

“Did you finally win a round of poker?” he asked, noting the smile on Shastri’s face. That was a rare sight.

"I don't think I will," Shastri replied, still smiling.

Ishan huffed, continuing to rummage through the racks. "Why bother with it then? Shouldn't you be planning for the next season already?”

Shastri hummed, his eyes drifting to the framed photo of him with his boys taken on the night they won.

“You should really clean your office, coach,” Ishan added, wrinkling his nose at the trail of dust in the corner.

“Yeah, I will.”

Ishan frowned, expecting the usual sarcastic reply. He shook his head to himself, letting it slide. No one knew what the old codger was thinking.

It was only later that night when Mayank quietly informed him about Shastri's retirement that Ishan realized how far he had come. How far they all had come. He noticed the extra inches in Mayank's hair, the peeling corners of the posters in their room, and how muchheused to long for everything to be over.

“Time will pass anyway,” Aditi told him. She did not talk about the part that said, “And you can do nothing about it.”

Ishan stayed up all night shortlisting new players so that the old grump could have an easy retirement – play poker and smoke weed or whatever old grumps did. By the time he finished, Mayank had fallen asleep over his pile of assignments. After circling the name of the new captain on the list, he got up to guide his sleepy friend to bed.

Ishan glanced at his jersey hanging on the door. Tradition dictated that the graduating captain would pass the number down to the next captain. Ishan smiled to himself before shutting his eyes.

"Good luck, Tillu.”

Title: CH: 26
Chapter_Text:
"What do you want?"

“You look like shit,” Mayank said, barging into the room. He shoved the sandwiches into Abhishek’s hands, his eyes scanning the messy room. "You haven't slept, have you?”

Abhishek ran a hand through his unruly hair before taking a swig of his Redbull. “The last time I slept, I had to leaveMeridian,” he said with a snort.

Mayank wrinkled his nose, snatching the Red Bull from him. "Eat something," he gestured towards the sandwiches.

“I don't remember asking you to run errands for me,” Abhishek muttered, unwrapping one for himself.

“I don't have a paper tomorrow,” Mayank said with a shrug. “And I care about you.”

Abhishek paused mid-chew, staring at Mayank. “Don’t make me lose my appetite,” he groaned.

Mayank chuckled, rubbing the back of his neck. "Um, goodnight," he muttered after a minute of awkwardly standing there.

“Where are you going?”

“To my room…?”

"You can stay," Abhishek huffed, catching Mayank off guard. Noticing, Abhishek quickly added, "Because I don't want to fall asleep, or I’d really fail this time.”

"Okay," Mayank mumbled, suppressing a smile. "I'll watch over you.”

Abhishek returned to highlighting his notes, giving Mayank a nod and motioning towards the half-eaten sandwich with his pen.

“What?” Mayank asked.

"My hands are busy," Abhishek replied, as if it were obvious.

Mayank bit the inside of his cheek, reminding himself to not laugh. He reached out and took the sandwich, gently feeding it to Abhishek, who glanced up at him, his expression shifting between annoyance and bashfulness.

They sat in comfortable silence for the next hour or two with Mayank scrolling through his phone and occasionally sneaking photos of Abhishek frowning adorably at his books.

“So,” Abhishek spoke first. “About what happened between us…”

“What happened between us?” Mayank asked, raising an amused brow.

Abhishek shot him a glare before clearing his throat. “You know, you told me that…”

“I like you,” Mayank completed. “What about it?”

Abhishek's cheeks flushed crimson. “So, are you going to do something about it or not?” he asked, his tone a mix of irritation and anticipation.

Mayank cautiously moved closer to Abhishek, testing the waters. “You nearly broke your arm last time, Abhi. Do you think you can handle it if I actually do something?”

To subside the sudden nervousness he felt, Abhishek flicked Mayank's forehead, causing the other to let out a small whine.

“See, this is exactly why I can'tdoanything,” Mayank said with a pout.

“That’s–!” Abhishek cursed as he flopped back onto his pillow. “That’s just how I am, I don't know what I want. Actually, you know what? You're wasting your time, Mayank.”

Mayank sighed and slowly lay down beside him. “What do you think I want?” he asked, causing Abhishek to turn and look at him.

“I don't know,” the other mumbled. “A relationship?”

“And you're scared of getting into one?”

“I’m not...scared,” Abhishek grunted. “Look, we’re not like Ishan and Shubman. We’re not the main characters in some love story where the universe is conspiring to bring them together. We’ll fade into an ordinary epilogue, and we’ll have to choose between living an ordinary life– with or without each other. There's no magic waiting for us.”

Mayank stared at him for a long time before smiling softly. “Then I’d choose an ordinary life with you.”

“What?” Abhishek’s eyes widened ever so slightly.

“Just because it's ordinary doesn't mean it's unimportant,” Mayank said with a grin. “Isn't that what Coach says? I’m an ordinary leg-spinner among thousand others, and I would probably never make it to the national team. Should I give up?”

“No, that's not–” Abhishek sat upright, his eyes fixed on Mayank. “It’s not the same.”

“I think it is,” Mayank said gently, resting a comforting hand on Abhishek's back. “We can't go on waiting for an epiphany. It doesn't have to be dramatic. Simple is okay, simple is beautiful. I just want to be with you. I don't care if we don't go on dates, or hold hands, or look at each other the way Ishan and Shubman do. Because we're not them, Abhi. We deserve our own story, and our story doesn't need to be magical. Our story should just feel like ours.”

Abhishek clenched his jaw, his mind reeling, but he kept his gaze locked on Mayank's. "I'm not exactly the romantic type," he blurted out.

"Me neither.”

"I'm not good at expressing my feelings.”

"I’m good at reading body language.”

Abhishek inched closer to Mayank without realizing. “What if we break up?”

Mayank grinned. “What if we don’t?”

Abhishek rolled his eyes, a faint smile tugging at his lips, before ruffling the other's hair.

“Padh le ab,” Mayank muttered. “Warna bolega maine fail karwa diya.”

“Please,” Abhishek scoffed. “I’m smarter than you even in my sleep.”

“Clearly not smart enough if you like me.”

Abhishek started to retort, then burst out laughing. “Did you just roast yourself?”

Mayank blinked. “I– Um.”

“Well, you proved your point at least,” Abhishek said before getting a pillow to his face.

Mayank was quite a doting boyfriend in his own way. He made sure Abhishek didn't neglect his health, brought him snacks to lift his mood on rough days, and listened attentively to his rants about nothing and everything. He’d send (un)funny memes at odd hours, leave ugly doodles on sticky notes at Abhishek's desk, and insist on spontaneous walks when Abhishek sat like a banana on his chair for hours.

"We can't have you wrinkling your pretty face," Mayank said, brushing the other's hair back with a grin.

Abhishek scrunched his nose. "What's with everyone calling me pretty these days?"

“Oh, really?” Mayank's eyebrows shot up. "And who else is?"

Abhishek flashed a small smirk before leaning back. "Guys, girls. You know, anyone with eyes really.”

Mayank blinked, then reached out to lightly furrow Abhishek's eyebrows with his fingers.

“What are you doing?”

“Trying to give you wrinkles.”

Abhishek swatted him away with a snort. So Mayank wasn't really the jealous type, Abhishek mentally crossed the box off his  bingo list.

Buthewas.

"So, this girl in my course was talking about you," Abhishek began.

"Yeah? What'd she say?" Mayank asked distractedly as he plugged in the PlayStation.

"You really want to know?"

"Sure," Mayank replied inattentively, just as he always did when Abhishek recounted details of his day.

Abhishek cleared his throat, pitching his voice high as he imitated, “Markande is a total candy! I just want to run my fingers through his hair and–”

Mayank fell onto the bed, doubling over with laughter. Abhishek rolled his eyes, crossing his arms. “Glad you find it funny.”

Mayank wiped away a tear, still chuckling. “Don’t you?”

“Oh,hilarious.”

“Wow, whoisthis girl?” Mayank laughed.

Abhishek smiled, crossing his arms. “You seem curious.”

Mayank immediately sealed his lips. “Uh, I mean, who is this girl?!” he said in mock anger. “Doesn't she know I have a gorgeous boyfriend?”

Abhishek snorted, shaking his head. "C'mere," he muttered, arms wide open.

It was rare for them to be physically affectionate. They were more like a middle school couple, content just to be in each other's presence. Yet, Mayank cherished the lingering gazes, subtle touches, and unspoken words just as much. He didn't want to rush things.

He smiled, gently holding Abhishek in his arms. "What's up?" he asked, running his hand through the other's hair.

“Nothing”, Abhishek mumbled, though his heart thumped in his chest.

Mayank pulled back slightly, gazing into Abhishek's hesitant eyes. “You can tell me–”

“I want to kiss you,” Abhishek confessed, his eyes trailing down to the other's lips.

Mayank's cheeks flushed. “W-What?”

“Can I?”

Mayank nodded weakly, allowing himself to be drawn closer until their lips met in a slow, tender kiss. It shouldn't have been surprising, considering they had been together for over a month, but Mayank still felt butterflies in his stomach. He hadn't expected Abhishek to make the move, so he was prepared to wait until the other showed signs of being comfortable.

When he regained his senses, he tugged Abhishek closer and lightly nibbled on his lip, asking for entrance. The latter let out a small gasp, letting him deepen the kiss. Their hands tangled in each other's hair as their bodies flushed together.

They pulled apart when Rishabh yelled that dinner was ready. "We should, um, go," Abhishek muttered, rubbing his neck.

"Yeah," Mayank whispered.

Their eyes met in the awkward silence before they burst into chuckles at the blush spreading across each other's faces.

Ishan cleared his throat, trying to get everyone's attention. “I have an important announcement–”

“Rishu, you fucker! That was my chicken!”

“Well, it's in my stomach now.”

“Guys–”

“Arrey koi wo pass karo mujhe.”

“Ketchup?”

“Arrey nahi yaar, wo jo saamne rakha hai.”

“Listen to me–”

“Tillu, bite your food, not me!”

“Can I bite you too, Abhi?”

“Shut up, Markande!”

“CAN YOU ALL NOT SHUT THE FUCK UP FOR ONCE?”

The silence that followed was only punctuated by Ishan's furious, heavy breathing. He let out an exasperated sigh, running his fingers through his hair.

"Jesus, it feels like I'm herding a group of toddlers," he grunted, rolling his eyes. "Anyways, I've got something to announce.”

He challenged anyone to interrupt, a satisfied smile playing on his lips as he noticed everyone's undivided attention. “So,” he added. “Before I send in the final list to the administration, I would like to unofficially declare our new captain.”

Everyone's eyes widened in excitement. “Wait, did you bring the jersey?” Rishabh asked, leaning forward.

"Of course," Ishan grinned as he unfolded the neatly pressed jersey, always marked with the number 1 for the captain.

“You’ve got to do the tradition,” Surya chimed in with a chuckle.

"Oh, right," Ishan cleared his throat, turning to address the first years. "You see, the first captain ofSummit'scricket team came from the volleyball squad, where, as you know, the number 1 belongs to the captain. He was just a benchwarmer in his own team, but had dreams of leading. Fast forward, he led us to the semi-finals of state championship in his third year, and since then, we hadn't made it past that.”

“Until this year, baby!”

“Yeah,” Ishan chuckled. “New captains are required to abandon their previous jersey numbers and accept the one passed on to them because we believe in the importance of sacrifice for the team. It is just the start. This number has been worn by both winning and losing captains, and I want you all to remember that no matter what, they never gave up.”

“I never gave up,” He turned to Tilak beside him. “Neither willyou.”

Tilak's eyes widened, mirroring the surprise on everyone else's face. Then, sudden cheers and hooting erupted in the room. Tilak’s eyes darted around nervously before settling on Ishan. “But bhaiya- I-”

“Welcome your new captain, Tilak Verma,” Ishan announced, his voice filled with pride and warmth.

“Tillu, bada aadmi ho gaya tu toh!”

“I feel like a proud father!”

“Everyone look away, I might cry.”

“You deserve it, Tillu!”

Tilak gulped, his eyes darting nervously to the happy faces around him. “No, no, no,” he muttered. “This has to be a prank, right? There's no way anyone in their right mind would choose me. There's no way I deserve this.”

Ishan's frown deepened, just as the others went silent, their expressions shifting from excitement to concern.

“Hey, what's wrong?” Ishan asked, placing a comforting hand on the other's back.

Tilak let out an abrupt laugh of disbelief. “Don't do this to me, Ishan bhaiya. Don't do this to the team. I’m… I’m a mess. I’ll ruin the glory you brought us.”

“Who are you to decide that?” Ishan's voice cut through the air, firm.

“You heard what my previous teammates said, right? I do not fit in–”

“Is that how you felt all this time?” Ishan said, slightly hurt. “Did we make you feel like you don't fit in?”

Tilak clenched his fists, fighting the urge to turn and run. “It's not you, it's me,” he mumbled.

Rohit clicked his tongue. “You know what? He's right. He doesn't fit in here.”

Tilak glanced up at the guy who dared him to speak, then quickly looked back down, feeling a surge of guilt wash over him.

Rohit added, “Because you don't need to fit in. This is your home, idiot. People don't need to fit in their own homes.”

Ishan shot him a small smile before turning to Tilak. “You wanna argue with that?”

Tilak bit his lip, shaking his head. “That's not what I meant, bhaiya. I love you guys, and that's exactly why I don't want to disappoint you–”

Rishabh landed a sharp slap on his back, causing him to squeal in surprise. “Kyu bey, tu jyotish hai kya?Did you foresee the future where you disappointed us?”

“But–”

“No buts!” Mayank said. “What did we learn about having some self confidence, Tillu?”

Ishan sighed. “Tilak, didn't you hear what I said about our first captain?”

Tilak frowned, tilting his head in confusion. Ishan added, “He was a benchwarmer in his old team. When he came here, he wasn't even a good player initially. How much better can the odds be than this? I don't make decisions lightly, Tilak. No one has practiced harder than you this season. Do you really want to give up without even trying?”

Tears welled up in Tilak's eyes as he shook his head firmly. “But bhaiya, I don't deserve—”

“Tilak,” Ishan interrupted, pulling him out of his thoughts. “Do youwantit?”

“Y-Yes.”

“Then fucking have it.”

Ishan handed the jersey to him, the number 1 prominently displayed on the back. Tilak hesitated for a moment, then accepted with trembling hands. As he held it, the weight of the moment sank in. The hopeful eyes on him carried more trust and belief than he had ever had in himself.

"You've got this, Tillu," Hardik said, grinning.

Tilak nodded weakly, managing to smile through his tears, letting himself believe for once.

"Yaar, phirse royega ye ab," Siraj said with a groan, prompting chuckles from the others.

Amidst the cheers and jeering, Tilak felt an encouraging pat on his shoulder. Ishan stared at him through his own tears that were starting to form.

“Take care of my home,” he whispered.

For the first time that evening, Tilak nodded without any hesitation. “I will, ofourhome.”

Title: CH: 27
Chapter_Text:
“How are you?”

Aditi felt a surge of bitterness at the warmth in his voice, the gentle creasing at the corners of his eyes, and the way they lit up with that painfully familiar spark as he drew closer to her.

"Ishan," she breathed his name like a gust of spring air.

He looked... beautiful, just as he did the last time she saw him. Two months of avoiding him seemed to crumble in an instant as he pulled her into a hug.

"How are you, Adi?” he asked again.

She smiled, averting her gaze. "Good," she nodded.

"Good," Ishan echoed, rubbing the back of his neck. “Um, you’ve got some time until you head back, right?”

Aditi fidgeted with the strap of her watch, debating what excuse to use. “Mom just called, actually, I need to–”

“Just a walk,” Ishan cut in. “For old time’s sake?”

Aditi’s eyes softened as she nodded, her resolve melting away. She had to remind herself that she could no longer reach out to hold his hand, slide their fingers together as they walked around aimlessly. You can forget a person, but how do you forget a habit?

“I think it's great,” she spoke. “Tilak being the new captain.”

Ishan smiled. “I know, right? That idiot underestimates himself.”

“Reminds me of you,” Aditi said with a small smile.

"Yeah," Ishan chuckled. "I've improved though, haven't I?"

"I've always believed in you," Aditi said, pausing before hesitantly ruffling his hair. "You will go on to live a great life, Ishan.”

And I won't be a part of it.

"I know it’s selfish to ask, but... you'll be there, won't you?" Ishan asked, glancing back at her.

"Where?"

"In my life," Ishan replied, letting out a short laugh. "I guess it wouldn't be that great without you.”

Aditi didn't want to cry, that was the only reason she looked away from him. “Yeah,” she mumbled. “Of course, I’ll be there.”

As if she could belong anywhere else.

Ishan shot her a warm smile as they strolled along. “We’re together,” he said after a while. “Me and Shubman.”

“Oh,” she muttered. “That's great! I’m so happy for you, Ish.”

Ishan grinned and high-fived her. “Turns out we’ve been together for years and didn’t know it,” he paused. “Um, sorry…”

Aditi waved dismissively. “Come on, we’re best friends, right? If anything, I’ll be waiting to hear all the gossip about yourboyfriend.”

Ishan giggled, and Aditi almost squeezed him with a hug. Instead, she settled for pulling his cheek. "I'll really miss you, Ish," she said.

"Don't talk like we'll never see each other again," Ishan groaned.

When Aditi just smiled, he insisted, "Wewillsee each other again, okay?"

"Okay," she muttered with a smile.

“You have to come to my first match when I get a contract.”

“Have you been attending the trials?”

Ishan nodded, a nervous yet hopeful glint in his eyes. “Scouts have been reaching out to Coach about me and the guys. I got a whiff that theMumbai Indiansare considering taking me on as a trainee to see how things work out.”

Aditi's eyes widened as a smile lit up her face. “Oh my god, Ish!” she squealed, habitually pulling him into a hug. “That's the best thing I’ve heard all day!”

Ishan beamed. “If I play well, maybe I can eventually get into the national team too. God, I don't know if I should be happy yet, but I really want to do this right, Adi. I need to repay everyone– Mom, Dad, my boys, andyou.”

Aditi rolled her eyes playfully. “Okay, then I want free tickets to your matches. Lifetime. Think you can swing that?”

“Your wish is my command, Ma’am,” Ishan laughed.

Aditi’s eyes softened as she looked at him. And in that moment, none of it mattered. It didn't matter that Ishan didn't love her back. It didn't matter that he was heralmost. It didn't matter that she would spend the rest of her life trying not to need him. All that mattered was that he was there, with her, despite everything.

No love, however brief, is ever wasted. She did not regret loving him, even though she knew that his name would be at the tip of her tongue when someday her daughter would have her first heartbreak. The hurt, however immense, was a receipt of her love. It was proof that she loved. She loved, and she had to pay the price. But she'd do it all over again in a heartbeat.

That was what it meant to love someone like Ishan. And there was a whole life in it, in knowing that youloved. Aditi concluded that heartbreaks were overrated. She believed that to love someone was to give them the permission to not love you back.

"Thank you," Aditi whispered. "For coming into my life.”

She finally found the closure she needed, the one that had kept her from moving on.

You love him, the story still ends.

“I’m so excited!” Dhruv exclaimed for what felt like the tenth time. “Aren't you?”

“Definitely not more than you,” Shreyas said with an amused chuckle.

“Yashu finally gets to be the captain. Of course, I’m excited,” Dhruv beamed. “You don't know how much he’s wanted this.”

Yashasvi, sitting beside him, offered a small smile. “We don't know for sure yet.”

Dhruv clicked his tongue. “Who else but you? Despite you being a brat to him, we all know how much Shub bhaiya trusts you.”

Yashasvi bit the inside of his cheek, his fingers fidgeting with each other. When Dhruv nudged him, he looked up with a deliberate frown. “I’m not a brat. Gill is a fucking–”

“The next word better be something nice,” Shubman chimed in as he flopped down next to the boys.

“Shouldn’t you be packing your bags and, I don't know,leave?”

Shubman clutched his chest dramatically. “Ouch. Cold as ever.”

Dhruv grinned, slinging an arm around Yashasvi. “We will really miss you, bhaiya. Won't you too, Yashu?”

Yashasvi simply snorted, causing Shubman to let out a deep sigh. “I’ll miss everyone too. Not Yashu though, you ungrateful cat!”

"What– I AM NOT A CAT!"

"Yes, you are. Those who get it, get it."

"You need to get some braincells.”

“And you need to get some bitches you little shi–”

"What are you guys even talking about?" Virat cut in, shooting them an annoyed look. "Come on, it's time for convocation.”

Shubman flashed a thumbs up, ruffling the younger ones' hair before fixing his tie as he rose. “You guys go ahead, I’m coming.”

Once the others left, Yashasvi cleared his throat. “Good luck with everything… bhaiya.”

Shubman’s eyebrows shot up in surprise as he slowly turned to face Yashasvi. A wide grin spread across his face, and the other groaned, realizing the teasing he had just invited upon himself.

“Did you just call me–”

“Shut up, Gill.”

Shubman giggled, jumping on the younger one’s back. “Oh, to think I’ve lived to hear you call me bhaiya. Can you say it again, I want to record it–”

"Get off, you're ruining my suit,” Yashasvi yelled, his cheeks flushing with embarrassment.

Shubman shook his head, reveling in the other's misery. “This is for being the most difficult teammate. Come on, to the left!”

Yashasvi groaned, but complied anyway. “You're heavy, fatass.”

“Can't even carry my weight, how will you bear the weight of captaincy? Shubman clicked his tongue.

Yashasvi paused, his jaw tightening in response. “I told you I–”

Shubman pressed a comforting hand on his shoulder as he got back down. “I know,” he said softly. “Sorry, muscle memory. Did you tell Dhruv?”

Yashasvi shook his head. “He’ll know by the next hour anyway.”

Shubman wanted to chide him for it, to warn Yashasvi about the hurt he might cause Dhruv, but seeing how tensed his body was, he held back. "If that's what you want," he sighed.

“Thank you, Captain,” Yashasvi whispered, finally smiling.

Shubman smiled in return, looking ahead. “Let's go, or I might cry.”

“So lame.”

“Shut up.”

“You shut up.”

Dhruv was grinning ear to ear, nudging his best friend excitedly as Shubman made his way onto the stage.

“Should we send a photo to Ishan bhaiya?”

Yashasvi scoffed. "I bet Gill had him on FaceTime while getting ready.”

“...With great honor and pride, we announceMr. Shubman Gill, captain of theMeridianCricket Team, as one of our distinguished graduates. His unwavering dedication, passion, and relentless drive for victory have propelled us to glory in the state championships twice under his leadership. We are grateful to have had the privilege of nurturing such prodigious talent within our ranks…”

“...We requestMr. Gillto do the honor of announcing the next captain in line and passing the torch to him…”

Dhruv turned to Yashasvi, slapping his back with enough force to make him stumble forward. “Go, hurry!”

Yashasvi closed his eyes, shaking his head.

Shubman smiled, gently tapping on the mic a few times before speaking. “It has been an absolute privilege to lead this team. As I step down from my role as the captain, I am honored to pass the mantle to a dear teammate and deserving successor,Mr. Dhruv Jurel.I have every confidence that Dhruv will lead with integrity, passion, and commitment to excellence, and I entrust him with the responsibility of leading our team to even greater heights. May our legacy soar in the meridian forever. Here's your captain signing off, goodnight and godspeed.”

Dhruv felt his throat parched. He didn't know how he managed to walk onto the stage and accept the sash from Shubman. Why was he smiling? Why was everyone clapping? Why was he here instead of Yashasvi? Dhruv's eyes found their way to his best friend, standing there with a smile. It looked strained, like he was sorry for something.

Something more than just this.

"We would also like to congratulateMr. Yashasvi Jaiswal," the host spoke. "He will soon commence his training at the NCA camp and contribute to the India A team. While it's with mixed emotions that we bid farewell to this exceptional young talent two years ahead of schedule, we fully support his decision to prioritize the start of his career above all else. Best of luck,Mr. Jaiswal. Remember, you're always welcome back home.”

Dhruv was happy. How could he not be? After all, this was what Yashasvi had always dreamed of – being in the front,leading. But this was better, wasn't it? The stars had to align for this moment. Dhruv was happier than anyone else could be for his best friend. But it still hurt. To have not heard it from Yashasvi himself. To feel soalonein front of hundreds of people.

"You knew?" he mumbled, glancing at Shubman, who nodded weakly.

"He's leaving," he added in another whisper. Shubman simply patted his back, leaving traces of sympathy behind.

Dhruv didn't deserve to feel betrayed. Yashasvi didn't owe him anything. He probably just wanted to surprise him. So, why did he look like he was about to cry?

"I think I’ve seen this before," Shubman sighed under his breath.

Shreyas glanced at Yashasvi and Dhruv, frozen in place like statues. “Let's hope your stupidity didn't rub off on Yashu,” he quipped.

“How dare you?” Shubman grumbled, taking a swig of his drink. As his phone buzzed, he took it out and smiled at the text. “I’ve got to be somewhere. Guess, I’ll head out early then?”

Shreyas nodded, clinking their glasses again. “Say hi to Ishan.”

Shubman grinned, waving. He spared one last glance at his two juniors before pointedly warning, “Make sure they don't fight, especially don't let one of themleave. Not everyone gets three years.”

“Huh?”

“Nothing.” Shubman gives a quick hug to him before walking away. “See you around.”

“Goodbye, Captain.”

Yashasvi rarely struggled with emotions. Probably because he didn't worry much about others' reactions. But with Dhruv, it was different. He cared for him, maybe even too much.

“Dhruv, I wanted to tell you–”

“Wait for me,” he breathed out, interrupting Yashasvi.

“What?”

Dhruv smiled. "Wait for me. In two years, I'll be at your level," he said. "I'll beat you someday.”

Yashasvi relaxed, a soft smile slowly gracing his lips. “Okay,” he muttered.

“And we will join the national team together.”

“Okay,” he repeated.

“And I’ll ask you out then.”

Yashasvi blushed furiously but managed to muster a weak, "Okay.”

Dhruv enveloped him in a tight hug, ruffling his hair gently. "So, wait for me," he whispered.

“I will,” Yashasvi promised.

“You deserve it, Yashu,” Dhruv said as he pulled away. “Go be the biggest star the world has ever seen.”

It was the first time Dhruv had seen his best friend smiling as wide as he was, and he wanted to freeze the time to savour it.

“You too, Captain.”

Title: CH: 29 (epilogue)
Chapter_Text:
December, 2022

The night before Ishan scored his double ton against Bangladesh, he couldn't sleep. He tossed and turned in his bed, his hand instinctively reaching out for Shubman, only to meet the cold mattress each time. Giving up on sleep, he stepped out onto the balcony. The cold air nipped at his skin, and his breath formed little clouds under the winter moon. The moon had always been his companion during his bouts of insomnia, especially in the absence of his boyfriend, who, might he add, was his very own portable moon.

Ishan smiled, the soft silver glow illuminating the gentle curve of his lips. Sometimes, it felt like a surreal dream how the world had taken off for him in the past three years. Getting scouted as a regular forMumbai Indians, his T20I debut, ODI debut– everything happened like a whirlwind. He often felt a supernova simmering within him, a familiar unrest preventing his bustling atoms from finding equilibrium. But he liked it. He liked that he always wanted to gobeyond. Besides, he had Shubman to instill some sense of accomplishment in his head from time to time.

Ishan pulled out his phone, his fingers driving him to his gallery as he fondly scrolled through the memories. Half of them were filled with dorky pictures of Shubman, and their impromptu dates. He chuckled as he came across a video of Shubman tripping down the stairs, rather embarrassingly in front of the whole team. Scrolling further down, Ishan grinned at the random spiral of photos Tilak and Dhruv had sent over the years, which rarely made sense. The rivalry betweenSummitandMeridiancontinued, although Hardik and Rishabh weren't satisfied with the updates, claiming the ‘fire’ had dwindled– whatever that meant.

Ishan blinked as a text notification popped up, wondering who would be online at such an hour. He smiled when he read Aditi’s good luck message.

thanksssadi

Why are you still up?

wdymim literally asleep

IshanGo to bedOr else

okay okayim not arguing with a womanwhatever you say beautiful ;)

…..I’m telling Shubman

Ishan chuckled, knowing she must be laughing too. He was glad they could still make each other smile. He was glad she let him stay in her life. He was glad for all he had gained and lost to be where he was today.

He sent her a goodbye before deciding to try sleeping again, the comforting weight of the happiness others wished for him settling on his chest. Even at his worst, he never had a reason strong enough to give up. It was no epiphany, but he always knew something in him would save him from utter ruin. That's just something he believed.

Shubman believed in Ishan. He believed in the man who leaped across the pitch in joy after scoring his first double century. Watching his boyfriend on the highlights for the third time in a row, Shubman couldn't help but tear up a little.

"So lame," Abhishek muttered, passing a tissue to Shubman.

"You're one to talk," Mayank scoffed, glancing at Abhishek, who was blinking aggressively to fight back tears.

“Shut up! I’m so proud of him!”

Mayank smiled, ruffling his boyfriend’s hair. “Yeah,” he mumbled, turning to the TV. “Me too.”

Shubman's cheeks were aching from the wide smile that stretched across his face, his heart swelling with love as he gazed at Ishan, who was shining brighter than the sun. “Guys, is this my sign to propose?”

Abhishek chuckled. "Didn't take you for someone to propose."

"Yeah, he'd just marry Ishan on a random Tuesday if he could," Mayank laughed.

Shubman smiled before sighing. "Yeah, as if that's ever going to happen. Not without losing our hard-earned careers.”

"If you disappear from the country, have an impromptu wedding in Venice within twenty-four hours, avoid joining the mile high club while returning,andget out without a trace of sex hair at the airport, it might just happen," Mayank joked.

Shubman rolled his eyes, tossing a piece of popcorn into his mouth. “Seriously though, I want to do something for him.”

“Bake him a cake or something,” Abhishek offered.

Mayank snorted. “Oh, he's already got one of those.”

“Watch your man, Abhi.”

Abhishek sighed, smacking both of them. “Let's just think of ideas, not that you two have the brains for that.”

Shubman pouted, turning back to the screen, which now showed a wild Rishabh jumping on Hardik's back before transitioning to Ishan's post-match interview. Shubman's ears perked up as he heard his boyfriend say, "Back home, everyone has been so supportive. This was for them. I really miss my friends. Maybe New Year's can be special, haha."

"Got it!" Shubman exclaimed, startling the other two. "Pack your bags, we've got places to be."

Abhishek blinked. "We're supposed to be planning something for Ishan, not for us, dude.”

"Call the boys," Shubman said, pulling out his phone from his pocket. "Tell Yashu he better come, or I’m dragging his ass from the NCA, I don't care.”

Mayank sighed. “I don't think he'll come, Shubman–”

“Tell him I’m getting married.”

“Are you?” Abhishek raised an eyebrow.

“No.”

Mayank shook his head, then rolled his eyes and texted Yashasvi anyway. “Shubman is getting married to some girl. Help me in murdering him. Sent.”

Shubman snorted. "What?" he asked Abhishek, who was eyeing him skeptically.

“What's on your mind, kake?”

Shubman grinned. “It's been too long, hasn't it?”

Abhishek smiled softly, nodding. “Yeah.”

Ishan found it odd that Virat was grinning from ear to ear throughout their entire flight. Especially odd when the other kept giggling while whispering something into Rishabh's ears, who also did the same. Ishan hated feeling left out of an inside joke, so he decided to ask Hardik about the matter.

“What’s everyone giggling about today?”

Hardik paused his music, frowning. “Man, I've been getting the lyrics all wrong. Apparently, it'sI love you the mostand notI love you toast.”

“Isn't that obvious?” Ishan deadpanned. “Ugh, Harry, listen to me!”

“Keep talking, Ishu baby,” the other muttered as he read the lyrics. “They should pronounce their Ms better–”

“Hardik!”

“Huh?” A confused Hardik finally turned his attention to Ishan.

Ishan huffed as Rishabh whispered something to Surya, and they both chuckled while glancing at him. “Is there something on my face? Why does everyone keep giving me funny looks?” he asked.

Hardik shrugged. “It's Shubman.”

“What?”

"Shubman said– mmph!"

Rahul quickly covered Hardik's mouth and flashed a tight-lipped smile at Ishan. "Shubman saidto have a safe journey. Now, if you'll excuse us, I'd like to have a talk with Harry.”

Ishan pouted, watching his last ray of hope being dragged away by his teammates. Since when did they start favoring Shubman over him? Sulking for the rest of the flight, Ishan eventually drifted off to sleep against the window.

"Are you dumb?" Rahul hissed.

"Sorry, I am!" Hardik yelped, his big brown eyes scanning the blank faces of the others.

Rahul blinked. "What?"

"I mean, I am sorry!" Hardik rephrased.

Rohit sighed. “Don’t just come at him, you all were being obvious enough.”

Virat pouted, slinging an arm around Rishabh. “Easy for you to keep quiet, you've been sleeping for hours! Normal people get bored, panda.”

Rohit waved them off before putting his eye mask back on. Surya cleared his throat, nodding towards a snoring Ishan. “So? How do we get him to the place without him knowing?”

“Kidnap,” Siraj said.

The others exchanged looks before shrugging. “Good idea,” they muttered before returning to their seats.

“How am I the dumb one?” Hardik grunted.

The designated trusted one to 'kidnap' Ishan was, of course, Rishabh. Kuldeep was supposed to blindfold him in a flash, while Virat was tasked with holding him down. Rohit was too tired to argue that Ishan would probably get traumatized.

Ishan still felt drowsy from his sleep as they walked out of the airport, dodging paparazzi by taking a private exit lane.

"Then I saw a naked dancing man," Surya said. He had been talking for the past few minutes, but Ishan was too sleepy to concentrate.

All his sleep vanished as his surroundings turned pitch black. Before he could react, his hands were bound behind his back. “What the–” a hand clasped over his mouth, filling him with dread.

The next hour or so was dark for Ishan. Literally. He grunted and groaned, trying to scream. He wanted to tell his idiot friends that he couldn't breathe. But silence swallowed him whole, and he was almost impressed at their newfound ability to shut the fuck up. Ishan knew the boys were up to something, and maybe it was connected to Shubman in a way. Well, at best, it was probably a surprise party– oldest trick in the book. They could have simply told him instead of attempting, and failing, at staging a low-budget kidnapping scene.

Rahul gestured to Rishabh to ease his grip on Ishan's mouth, but Rishabh, being himself, blinked in confusion before releasing his hold altogether.

Ishan gasped for a breather. “What do you want?” he tried to sound panicked.

Rishabh grinned before putting on his kidnapper voice. “Hand me all the Teuscher chocolates you got today!”

“What? How do you know about that?”

Rahul face-palmed as Rishabh smiled sheepishly. “Uhm, I have my resources! Shut yer trap and don't make trouble.”

Ishan chuckled. “You’re still terrible at imitating, Rishu.”

Hardik snorted from the other side of the car. “What was that about me being the dumb one again?”

“Shut up, Harry!”

Ishan sighed. “Can you at least free my hands? I can't see shit, where'd you even get a blindfold?”

The rest of the journey remained a mystery to Ishan, but at least he could breathe properly now. He tried to coax the others into revealing the ‘surprise’, but it was useless. Rishabh even turned down a bribe of chocolates, so they must be serious about it. Shubman had never successfully surprised Ishan. Ishan always found out and sometimes felt bad enough to pretend to be surprised, but Shubman’s shoulders would slump as he would pout and say, "You knew, didn't you?”

But this time, Ishan genuinely had no clue, aside from knowing he was being taken somewhere.

"All this money and you couldn't hire someone to blow up the balloons?" Abhishek grunted as he struggled to tie another balloon, his cheeks puffed out from exertion.

"I thought you'd be good at it," Shubman said with a smirk, high-fiving Mayank.

Abhishek's cheeks flushed as he kicked Shubman in the shin. “You guys are intolerable. I wish Ishan was here already.”

"He'll be here soon, so you'd better hurry up.”

Shubman sighed dramatically at the intruder's voice. "Who invited her again?"

“You did,” Aditi said, smacking him with the bundle of ribbons she held.

“Bitch,” Shubman muttered, rubbing his head.

“Self depreciation is not good, Shubman!” Aditi shot him a smile before pushing him the other way. “Go and get dressed. Rishabh said they’re almost here.”

Shubman cursed under his breath, tossing the scissors to Mayank as he hurried inside Virat's farmhouse they were using. He chose a fitted black t-shirt under a denim jacket, paired with distressed denim jeans. Poor Ishan was probably still in his travel jersey, so Shubman didn't want to look overdressed either.

He couldn't help but smile at the thought of finally hugging his love after being away for weeks. Shubham hummed as he strolled outside once again, spotting a beaming Dhruv and an awkwardly waving Yashasvi. Tilak was nearby, accompanied by his boyfriend whose name Shubman couldn't quite recall. White Boy, close enough.

Shubman grinned, slinging an arm around Yashasvi. “Glad to know you're still alive, my favourite jun– ouch!”

Yashasvi scoffed, punching the other in the stomach. “Can't say I feel the same about you being alive.”

“NCA couldn't change this guy,” Shubman wiped away fake tears. “Oh, Yashu, I missed you and your knack for being a complete asshole so much!”

Yashasvi scrunched his nose, taking a swig of his drink. Shubman noticed Yashasvi and Dhruv's shoulders touching and raised an eyebrow at Dhruv questioningly. If there was one thing to know about Yashasvi, it was that he despised skinship, or at least pretended to.

Dhruv sighed and shook his head. Shubman nodded knowingly, mouthing, ‘he's shy.’ Yashasvi coughed, looking around as he said, “They're here.”

Shubman turned around eagerly, watching as a blindfolded Ishan was escorted in by the other guys. Shubman chuckled, rushing into the arms of his boyfriend, who, despite being confused for a second, melted into the familiar touch immediately.

“Baby,” he whispered into Ishan's ears. “I missed you.”

"I missed you too," Ishan mumbled, planting a chaste kiss on the other's neck. "But can I remove this thing now, for fuck’s sake?" he added with a groan, tugging at the blindfold.

Shubman hummed. “Maybe you should keep it on until we get into bed–”

“Don't make me beat you up in front of everyone.”

"Alright, then," Shubman chuckled, reaching out to remove the blindfold.

Ishan's eyes took a second to adjust after being in the dark for over an hour. But seeing his boyfriend's face up close was the best sight of all. After all, video calls could only suffice so much. His hand instinctively reached to caress Shubman's cheek, who leaned into the touch. Ishan was pretty sure his eyes showed disgustingly fond affection, but he couldn't care less as he quickly pecked the other's lips.

Shubman was taken aback for a moment, knowing Ishan wasn't usually into PDA, but he guessed the latter was probably just overwhelmed. Shubman smiled, ruffling Ishan's hair before placing a kiss on his forehead.

Rishabh cleared his throat from beside them. "I think we should get the party started. Virat's starting to miss Anushka, seeing you two lovebirds. Unless you want him to go on a two-hour speech about how much he loves her, let's go?”

Ishan finally looked around, his eyes widening at the beautifully decorated backyard. But what almost brought tears to his eyes was seeing his friends after months, especially Aditi.

He rushed to hug Tilak, ruffling his hair. Hardik and Rishabh followed, pouncing on the poor guy. "Tillu, you got a new hairstyle! How dare you not update us on it?" Rishabh exclaimed, causing Tilak to laugh along with the others.

“I missed you guys,” Tilak said with a grin.

Surya clicked his tongue. “Says the one who doesn't have time anymore to see us.”

“We literally spent the entire IPL together, bhai!”

Ishan left them to bicker, turning to Aditi, who greeted him with her usual warm smile. It had been almost a year since Ishan last saw her, as she couldn't attend the IPL due to her fashion gigs. So he shrugged off his hesitation and hugged her.

Aditi would never get over how comforting it was to hug Ishan. It was like being wrapped in all things warm and fluffy. "How are you, Ish?" she asked.

"Never been better," Ishan replied with a smile as they pulled back from the hug.

Aditi nodded with a small smile, looking down. Could she say that she missed him? She probably shouldn't.

Shubman cleared his throat, slinging an arm around each of them. "She missed you," he chimed in. "Not more than me though.”

Aditi blinked, glancing at Shubman who flashed her a smile. She chuckled and ruffled his hair. "I'll go get the cake ready.”

Ishan’s eyes lit up. “Cake? Yo, you're not too bad at this, huh?” he nudged Shubman, who grinned adorably.

"He has been of no help all day," Yashasvi huffed, joining them.

Ishan hugged him and Dhruv warmly, commenting on how much they've grown since the IPL.

"Thanks for your input, Yashu," Shubman quipped with a grin. "I'll be disregarding it along with a complimentary shut the fuck up.”

Yashasvi shot him a blank stare before turning to Ishan. “You were really cool that day.”

“We’re so proud of you,” Dhruv added.

Ishan chuckled. “Thanks guys. I can't wait for us to play together–”

“Rishabh’s trying to eat all the cream, hurry up guys!”

Much later, after Aditi convinced all the boys to pair up for a slow dance because it would be funny, Ishan found a quiet moment to look up at the stars painted across the sky. The gentle breeze carried the faint laughs of his friends and he couldn't help but smile.

"What's your pretty head thinking about?" Shubman whispered, his hand wrapped around the other's waist as they swayed slowly.

Ishan looked up at his boyfriend with a soft chuckle. “You.”

Shubman blinked. “Are you not well?”

“Die.”

“Now that's more like it,” Shubman grinned.

"Do you ever wonder," Ishan said, licking his lips. "What if we never fought back then?"

Shubman hummed. "I’d have never known how much I can't survive without you.”

Ishan smiled, kissing the other's cheek. “I love you.”

It never got old, no matter how many times he said it. Those three words were never going to get worn out, like a ragged cloth that had been a testament to enduring changes. To be loved meant to be changed. Looking back at 17-year-old Ishan– insecure and angry at the world– now his edges were softened, shaped by Shubman's unending love and his own self acceptance.

"I love you more," Shubman whispered, his dimples forming little crescents as he melted into Ishan's gaze.

On the other side of the party, Abhishek puffed his cheeks, flushing crimson as they swayed in a slow dance. "This is so embarrassing."

"Hm?" Mayank beamed, shooting a thumbs up at his friends who were hyping him up from behind. "It's been three years, babe. Get used to it.”

“Don't call me that!”

In the distance, Yashasvi snorted, swirling his champagne. Dhruv nudged him, asking, “Why are we not dancing?”

Yashasvi blinked, quietly sipping his drink. “Because we're not a… couple.”

“We could be.”

Yashasvi could feel the hot liquid gliding down his throat all of a sudden. “Once we join the national team,” he mumbled.

"Right," Dhruv smiled. "I heard you'll debut in the Test team later in the year.”

“Probably,” Yashasvi shrugged. “There's still time for that.”

“Yashu.”

Yashasvi looked up from his drink to find Dhruv smiling at him. "Too bright," he mumbled to himself.

"I'm proud of you. I hope you know that."

Yashasvi let out a breath, his eyes trailing to the laughing faces of his seniors. "Yeah," he smiled. "I'm proud too. Of us.”

After taking a bold sip, Yashasvi added, "Let's dance.”

Dhruv blinked in surprise. "But we're not a–"

"So aren't they," Yashasvi interrupted, gesturing towards a drunk Rishabh trying to get Rohit into dancing while Rohit cursed loudly at everyone.

Dhruv laughed, nodding as they joined the dance floor together. Well, Yashasvi didn't exactly mean slow dancing; instead, he showed off his weird moves. Dhruv chuckled, and danced with him anyway because, well, he was his best friend after all.

Aditi was making a drink for herself when everyone erupted into cheers. She looked up to see Ishan and Shubman kissing. Oddly enough, she felt… relieved. She had moved on a long time ago, but a small part of her was afraid to meet the truth head-on. Seeing the widest smile on her first love’s face and seeinghisfirst love reciprocate with equal love felt like an epiphany.

"Hey," Abhishek spoke, interrupting her internal monologue. "The ice," he pointed at the melting ice in the scoop.

“Oh,” she muttered, reaching for another cube. “They look lovely together,” she said with a chuckle.

"Yeah," Abhishek hummed. "Do you still think about him?" he asked after a pause.

"No," Aditi replied with a smile, dropping an ice cube into Abhishek's drink as well. "I've managed to keep myself busy."

Abhishek grinned. "Strong, independent woman and all that, huh?”

She chuckled, nodding. Abhishek thanked her for the ice and then turned to look at his best friends, who were now giggling like newlyweds.

“What about you?”

"Hm?" Abhishek smiled at the sight of Shubman picking up Ishan in a bridal style, causing the latter to kick his legs in protest.

“Do you still think about him?”

Abhishek turned to her, his smile unwavering. “No,” he said. “Mayank is a better bathroom singer anyway.”

Aditi smiled, raising her glass as they clinked. Soon enough, Virat called everyone together for a group photo– much to Tilak's excitement, despite having red wine running down his white shirt. Ishan couldn't stop smiling as if it were his last day on Earth. Maybe it was. He didn't regret a single moment of his life, even if he were to die the next day. The gods must envy him. They envy him because he's mortal, and everything is more beautiful because he would never be here again. Nothing hurts anymore.

He wished he could tell that to his 17-year-old self, who was probably crying himself to sleep in his dark room, blaming himself for everything wrong in the world. Ishan wanted to tell him, to promise him that yes,it gets better.

As the camera flashed, Ishan kissed Shubman's cheek while the others struck silly poses, their soft chuckles and unbridled laughs ringing freely in the air.

"What do you like about life?"his Coach had asked when Ishan first joined the national team.

"That it goes on,"he had answered.

♡₊˚ 🦢・₊✧

a/n:thank you for everything guys, i’ll talk to you in the ending notes :)

Title: thank yous & goodbyes
Chapter_Text:
first of all, once again, thanks for sticking with me through another story. your responses have been so heartwarming for someone who was writing full-length stories for the first time. i never really thought ofIOIIYblowing up, and still receiving so much love forrematch; all i can say is thank you. you all have made my inner child so happy during all this time haha.

rematchwill always remain close to my heart because it's something i actually wanted to write. even thoughIOIIYis also very memorable, i wasn’t very satisfied with it in terms of a plot.rematchwas as close as i could get to satisfying myself, and feel a little proud about it. i got so used to the characters that sometimes i forget they’re real. like what do you mean summit and meridian are not real? i got really used to those names as well haha. it’s a little... odd, at the very least to end the story i had so much fun writing. But, well, it had to end someday.

i’d like to heard you all’s thoughts, theories (because i did drop a bomb on y’all in the epilogue lol), questions, and favourite characters, etc. or we could just talk. i think this time, there's a lot to choose your favourite character from. mine is ishan obviously, that's by nature. but other than that, it’s yashasvi. my little tsundere pookie.

now, coming to my final note. i’ve told you guys about this already but that doesn't make it any easier haha. this was most definitely my last story and i will be leaving wattpad. im sorry, i know i promised you guys to rethink about it but well, it’s just what feels right to do. it’s not that i’ve grown out of ishman or anything like that, i probably wasn’t very serious of them to begin with. and it’s not over any other issue either, it’s a very standalone decision. i've been in and out of fandoms all my life, and im actually impressed that i managed to stick here so long. all thanks to you lovely people! i earned some great friends from here, and i’ll be grateful for that.

i won’t be deleting any of my works and my account will remain here as an archive. i might log in sometimes, but honestly, i hadn’t used wattpad since 8th grade until last november so i can't promise anything. i most definitely wouldn't be writing again so please don't make me emotional after this haha. i’ll be checking in for your comments and notes until i delete the app, which i plan to do before leaving for college in july. im actually starting college this year! im excited about finally getting rid of chem, and getting to study physics all i want.

i’ll always love ishan. heisthe sun for me, and i hope he achieves all the greatness in the world and beyond. as long as i watch cricket, i’ll always be supporting him.

thanks for reading my stories, thanks for always being so supportive. writing was just a neglected hobby before you all.

be kind to yourself and others. live your life, love your life. never give up. goodnight and godspeed.
